A001.name: Shelter
A001.text: You can immediately build a stable at no cost, but only if you place it in a pasture covering exactly 1 farmyard space. (Cost 2F.) Immediately plow 1 field.

A003.name: Paper Knife
A003.text: Select 3 occupations in your hand and determine one of them at random. You can immediately play that occupation, without paying an occupation cost.

A004.name: Baseboards
A004.text: You immediately get 1 wood for each room you have. If you have more rooms than people, you get 1 additional wood. (Cost 1F.) You immediately get 1 clay for every 2 clay you already have in your supply.

A006.name: Storage Barn
A006.text: If you have the Joinery, Pottery, Basketmaker's Workshop, or Well, you immediately receive 1 wood, 1 clay, 1 reed, and 1 stone, respectively.

A007.name: Garderner's Knife
A007.text: You immediately receive 1 food for each grain field and 1 grain for each vegetable field you have.

A008.name: Food Basket
A008.text: You immediately receive 1 grain and 1 vegetable. (Cost 1 Sheep.) You immediately get 1 cattle. (Effectively, you are exchanging 1 sheep for 1 cattle.)

A010.name: Wooden Shed
A010.reason: Wooden Shed grants room for a person but its cost is 'you cannot renovate anymore' — no engine hook can block a future renovate action, so the drawback can't be enforced (implementing only the benefit would misrepresent the card)
A010.text: This card can only be played as a major improvement. It provides room for 1 person. For that, you cannot renovate anymore.

A011.name: Mud Patch
A011.reason: Mud Patch's core ability is holding 1 wild boar on each unplanted field tile — animal placement validation (state.validate_animal_placement) has no concept of field cells as animal storage; would need engine changes
A011.text: When you play this card, you immediately receive 1 wild boar. You can hold 1 wild boar on each of your unplanted field tiles. (Cost 1C.) Each of your pastures (with or without a stable) can hold up to 2 more animals.

A013.name: Renovation Company
A013.prereq_text: in a wooden house with exactly 2 rooms
A013.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 3 clay. Immediately after, you can renovate without paying any building resources.

A014.name: Carpenter's Hammer
A014.text: Each time you build at least 2 wood/clay/stone rooms at once, you get a total discount of 2 reed as well as 2 wood/3 clay/4 stone.

A015.name: Carpenter's Axe
A015.text: Each time after you use a wood accumulation space, if you then have at least 7 wood in your supply, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 wood. When you play this card, you immediately get 1 clay. You can use clay instead of wood to build fences.

A017.name: Reclamataion Plow
A017.reason: Reclamation Plow triggers after animals are 'accommodated' following an accumulation-space take, but the accommodate action has no fire() event — nothing hooks the resolution of an accommodate prompt
A017.text: After the next time you take animals from an accumulation space and accommodate all of them on your farm, you can plow 1 field.

A018.name: Wheel Plow
A018.reason: Wheel Plow needs 'first person placed this round' plus a once-per-game flag plus a 2-cell follow-up choice, triggered off the 'plow' event; the plow ctx carries only the single cell just plowed with no action-space id, and chaining a reliable multi-cell choice on top of that is too fragile to implement with confidence
A018.text: Once this game, when you use the "Plow 1 field" or "Plow 1 field and/or Sow" action space with the first person you place in a round, you can plow 2 additional fields. (Cost 1W.) Add 5 to the current round and place 1 field tile on the corresponding round space. At the start of that round, you can plow the field.

A020.name: Double-Turn Plow
A020.text: When you play this card, you can immediately plow up to 2 fields. If you play this in Round 3 or before, you immediately receive 1 food.

A021.name: Family Friendly Home
A021.text: Each time you take a "Build Rooms" action while having more rooms than family members already, you also receive a "Family Growth" action and 1 food.

A022.cost_text: 2F Req at least 1 fence in supply
A022.name: Telegram
A022.reason: Telegram places a guest as an additional person for a round — guest tokens / extra people aren't supported
A022.text: Add 1 to the current round for each fence in your supply and place 1 guest on the corresponding round space. In that round, you must place the guest as an additional person.

A023.name: Stone Company
A023.text: Immediately after each time you use a "Stone" Accumulation space, you get a "Major or Minor Improvement" action during which you must spend at least 1 Stone.

A025.name: Bassinet
A025.reason: Bassinet lets you place an additional person on an occupied action space — guest tokens / placing on occupied spaces aren't supported
A025.text: Each work phase, you can place a(nother) person on the first non-accumulating action space used by any player (including you), as long as there is only 1 person on that space.

A027.name: Oven Site
A027.text: When you play this card, you receive 2 wood and you can immediately build the major improvement Clay Oven A5 or Stone Oven A6 . Either way, it only costs you 1 clay and 1 stone.

A028.name: Forest School
A028.reason: Forest School's two clauses (treat occupation spaces as unoccupied; pay occupation cost in wood instead of food) are both unsupported: placing on occupied spaces is on the not-supported list, and occupation cost payment has no substitution channel
A028.text: You can consider "Occupation" action spaces not occupied. You can replace each food that an occupation costs with wood.

A029.name: Ale-Benches
A029.text: In the returning home phase of each round, you can pay exactly 1 grain from your supply to get 1 bonus point. If you do, each other player gets 1 food.

A030.name: Baking Sheet
A030.text: Each time you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can use this card to exchange exactly 1 grain for 2 food and 1 bonus point.

A031.name: Debt Security
A031.text: During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each Major improvement you have, up to the number of your unused farmyard spaces. (Cost 2W.) During scoring, if your pastures cover at least 6/7/8/10 farmyard spaces, you get 1/2/3/4 bonus points. (Req all farmyards spaces used.) For each complete round left to play, you immediately get 1 bonus point and 2 food.

A034.name: Loppers
A034.reason: Loppers spends '1 fence in your supply' (a future fence-building allowance) alongside 1 wood — the engine has no concept of a per-player consumable fence-piece pool distinct from the wood cost of building fences
A034.text: Each time you build 1 or more fences, you can also use this card to exchange 1 wood and 1 fence in your supply for 2 food and 1 bonus point.

A035.name: Swimming Class
A035.text: In the returning home phase of each round, if you return a person from the "Fishing" accumulation space, you receive 2 bonus points for each newborn that you return home.

A036.name: Facades Carving
A036.text: When you play this card, you can exchange 1 food for 1 bonus point, up to the number of completed harvests.

A037.name: Bucksaw
A037.text: Each time you renovate, you can also pay 1 wood to get 1 bonus point and 1 grain. (Req 5 Sheep.) During scoring, if you live in a wooden/clay/stone house by then, you get 3/2/0 bonus points.

A039.name: Chapel
A039.reason: Chapel: card_space (engine phase 9) now lets a card become an action space open to all players, and the 'another player must first pay you 1 grain' toll is squarely the I337 toll-to-owner pattern. But 'a player who uses it gets 3 bonus points' must credit points to WHICHEVER PLAYER PLACES there, not just Chapel's owner -- and the engine's only bonus-points channel is score_bonus(state, player, inst), which cards.score_bonuses only ever evaluates over a player's OWN in-play cards (in_play(player)). A card living in the owner's minors has no way to credit points to a different (non-owner) placer's score, and cards.grant_goods -- the channel a card_space resolve fn uses to pay a toll to someone who isn't the acting player -- only carries physical resources/animals, never abstract bonus points. Registering only the owner's-own-placement half and silently dropping the cross-player bonus would violate fidelity, so this stays UNIMPLEMENTED until a per-player bonus-points channel independent of card ownership exists.
A039.text: This is an action space for all. A player who uses it gets 3 bonus points. If another player uses it, they must first pay you 1 grain.

A040.name: Potter's Yard
A040.text: Immediately place 1 clay on each unused space in your farmyard. As soon as you use a space, you receive the clay and you can immediately exchange it for 2 food.

A041.name: Vegetable Slicer
A041.text: Each time you upgrade a Fireplace to a Cooking Hearth, you immediately receive 2 wood and 1 vegetable (not retroactively).

A042.name: Forest Lake Hut
A042.text: Each time you use the "Fishing"/"Take 3 Wood" accumulation space, you also receive 1 wood/food.

A043.name: Farmyard Manure
A043.text: Each time you build 1 stable during an action, place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

A045.name: Fire Protection Pond
A045.text: As soon as you do not live in a wooden house anymore, place 1 food on each of the next 6 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

A046.name: Claw Knife
A046.text: Each time you use the "Take 1 Sheep" accumulation space, place 1 food on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

A047.name: Trellises
A047.text: Immediately place 1 food on each of the next round spaces, up to the number of fences you built. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

A048.name: Shaving Horse
A048.text: Each time you obtain 1 wood, if afterward you have at least 5/7 wood in your supply, you can/must exchange 1 wood for 3 food.

A049.name: Nest Site
A049.reason: Nest Site's head effect triggers on reed being added to a non-empty Reed Bank during the preparation-phase replenishment; that replenishment isn't a hookable event (round_start fires after it already happened, with no 'was it empty before' information)
A049.text: Each time 1 reed is placed on a non-empty "Take 1 Reed" accumulation space during the preparations phase, you receive 1 food. (Cost 1C.) Each time any player (including you) uses the "Cattle Market" accumulation space, you get 3 food, and each other player gets 1 food.

A051.name: Drift-Net Boat
A051.text: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you receive an additional 2 food.

A052.name: Throwing Axe
A052.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space while the "Take 1 Wild boar" accumulation space has at least 1 wild boar, you also receive 2 food. (Cost 1C.) In the returning home phase of each round, if you gained at least 7 building resources in the preceding work phase, you get 2 food.

A054.name: Credit
A054.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 5 food. At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you must pay 1 food, or else take a begging marker. (Cost 1W 1C.) Each time after you build an improvement, including this one, you get 1 food. (Cost 1R.) Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can exchange 2 wood for 3 food. If you do, place those 2 wood on the accumulation space.

A057.name: Milking Parlor
A057.text: When you play this card, if you have at least 1/3/4 sheep, you receive 2/3/4 food. The same applies for 1/2/3 cattle.

A058.name: Asparagus Knife
A058.text: In the returning home phases of rounds 8, 10, and 12, you can take 1 vegetable from exactly 1 vegetable field. You can immediately exchange it for 3 food and 1 bonus point.

A059.name: Potato Ridger
A059.text: Each time after you harvest at least 1 vegetable, if you then have at least 3/4 vegetables in your supply, you can/must exchange 1 vegetable for 6 food.

A060.name: Oriental Fireplace
A060.text: At any time, you can turn goods into food. You receive 4 food per vegetable, 3 food per sheep, and 5 food per cattle. Each time you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can turn grain into 2 food each.

A061.name: Winnowing Fan
A061.text: After the field phase of each harvest, you can bake exactly 1 grain. (This is not considered a "Bake Bread" action.)

A062.name: Beer Keg
A062.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to exchange 1/2/3 grain for 0/1/2 bonus points and exactly 3 food.

A064.name: Barley Mill
A064.text: In the field phase of each harvest, you get 1 food for each grain field that you harvest.

A065.name: Seed Pellets
A065.text: Each time before you take a "Sow" action, you receive 1 grain.

A066.name: Feeding Dish
A066.text: Each time you use an animal accumulation space while already having an animal of that type, you receive 1 grain. (Cost 1W.) Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you get 1 additional grain.

A068.name: Asparagus Gift
A068.reason: Asparagus Gift compares fences built in one action to the current round number, but the fences_built ctx carries only 'new_pastures' (completed pasture cell-groups), not the count of fence edges built this action
A068.text: Each time you build a number of fences equal to or greater than the current round, you immediately get 1 vegetable. (Cost 2W. Req 2 occ.) Add 4, 7, and 9 to the current round and place 1 vegetable on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the vegetable.

A070.name: Lifting Machine
A070.text: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can move 1 vegetable from one of your fields to your supply. (This is not considered a field phase.) (Cost 1W.) At any time, you can move 1 crop from a planted field containing at least 2 crops to an empty field.

A072.name: Calcium Fertilizers
A072.text: Each time you use a stone accumulation space, add 1 additional good of the respective type to each of your planted fields growing a single type of crop.

A073.name: Agricultural Fertilizers
A073.text: Each time after you turn at least 2 unused spaces into used spaces in one action, you get an additional "Sow" action.

A074.name: Stable Tree
A074.text: Each time you build at least 1 stable on your turn, place 1 wood on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the wood.

A076.name: Cob
A076.text: At the start of each work phase, you can exchange exactly 1 clay plus 1 grain for 3 clay plus 1 food.

A077.name: Hod
A077.text: When you play this card, you immediately receive 1 clay. Each time any player (including you) uses the "Take 1 Wild boar" accumulation space, you immediately receive 2 clay. (1VP. Cost 2W. Req 1 occ.) Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get an additional 1 food and 1 reed.

A079.name: Garden Hoe
A079.text: Each time you take a "Sow" action planting vegetables in at least 1 field, you receive 1 clay and 1 stone. (Cost 1W.) Each time you use a stone accumulation space, you get 1 additional stone.

A081.name: Interim Storage
A081.text: Each time you use a clay/reed/stone accumulation space, place 1 wood/clay/reed on this card. At the start of rounds 7, 11, and 14, move all the goods on this card to your supply.

A082.name: Work Certificate
A082.text: Each time after you use an action space, you can take 1 building resource from a building resource accumulation space with at least 4 building resources on it.

A084.name: Silage
A084.text: In each returning home phase after which there is no harvest, you can pay exactly 1 grain - even from a field - to breed exactly one type of animal.

A085.name: Homekeeper
A085.reason: extra room capacity depends on live farmyard adjacency (field + pasture); extra_rooms is a flat per-card int, not a dynamic geometry-based predicate.
A085.text: Exactly one room in your clay or stone house can hold an additional family member if it is adjacent to both a field and a pasture.

A089.name: Stable Planner
A089.text: Add 3, 6, and 9 to the current round and place 1 stable on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds (not earlier), you can build the stable at no cost.

A091.name: Shifting Cultivator
A091.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can also pay 3 food to plow 1 field.

A093.name: Bed Maker
A093.text: Each time you add rooms to your house, you can also pay 1 wood and 1 grain to immediately get a "Family Growth with Room Only" action.

A094.name: Lazy Sowman
A094.reason: requires placing a person on an occupied action space (guest-token style); not supported.
A094.text: Each time you decline a "Sow" action on your turn, you can immediately place another person on an Action space of your choice (even if it is occupied).

A095.name: Angler
A095.text: Each time after you use the "Fishing" accumulation space while there are at most 2 food on that space, you get a "Major or Minor Improvement" action.

A096.name: Task Artisan
A096.reason: needs a 'stage card revealed in the preparation phase' hook (none exists) and grants a Minor Improvement action (engine-internal, not exposed).
A096.text: When you play this card and each time a stone accumulation space is revealed (and placed on a round space) in the preparations phase, you receive 1 wood and a "Minor Improvement" action.

A097.name: Freshman
A097.reason: requires intercepting/replacing the Bake Bread action with playing an occupation; no hook fires before Grain Utilization/Farmland/Cultivation resolves a bake.
A097.text: Each time you receive a "Bake Bread" action, instead of taking the action, you can play an occupation, without paying an occupation cost.

A099.name: Fellow Grazer
A099.text: During the scoring, you receive 2 bonus points for each of your pastures covering at least 3 farmyard spaces.

A10.name: Basketmaker's Workshop
A10.reason: available as the built-in major improvement
A10.text: In each harvest, you can use the Basketmaker's Workshop to convert exactly 1 reed to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/5 reed.

A100.name: Curator
A100.reason: requires a 'returning home phase' hook and tracking which spaces were accumulation-type per player this round; occupied_by/placement counts are reset before any card hook fires.
A100.text: In the returning home phase of each round, if you return at least 3 people from accumulation spaces, you can buy 1 bonus point for 1 food.

A101.name: Cookery Outfitter
A101.text: During the scoring, you receive 1 bonus point for each (cooking) improvement you have.

A103.name: Portmonger
A103.text: Each time you take 1/2/3+ food from a food accumulation space, you also receive 1 vegetable/grain/reed.

A104.name: Wood Harvester
A104.text: In the field phase of each harvest you receive 1 wood for each wood accumulation space with exactly 2 wood. In the field phase of each harvest you receive 1 food for each wood accumulation space with 3+ wood.

A105.name: Barrow Pusher
A105.text: For each new field tile you get, you also get 1 clay and 1 food.

A106.name: Slurry Spreader
A106.text: In the field phase of each harvest, each time you take the last grain/vegetable from a field, you also get 2/1 food.

A107.name: Catcher
A107.text: Each time you place your 1st/2nd/3rd person in a round on a building resource accumulation space with exactly 5/4/3 building resources, you get 1 food. (1-5 players) Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can exchange 1 wood for 2 food. If you do, place the wood on the accumulation space.

A109.name: Small Trader
A109.reason: minor_played doesn't carry which action space triggered it, so 'via the Major/Minor Improvement action' (vs. Meeting Place) can't be distinguished; the DB text for this code is also visibly concatenated with several unrelated cards.
A109.text: Each time you take a "Major or Minor Improvement" action to play an improvement from your hand, you also get 3 food. (1-5 players) Each time you build at least 1 clay room or renovate your house from clay to stone, you also get 3 food. (1-5 players) Each time you build at least 1 room, you can place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (1-5 players) When you play this card, you immediately get 1 grain. In the field phase of each harvest, you can harvest 1 additional grain from each of your grain fields.

A113.name: Heresy Teacher
A113.reason: requires a field to simultaneously hold two crop types (grain stacked with vegetable); the field model stores a single crop type per cell/card.
A113.text: Each time you use the "Occupation" action space, you receive 1 vegetable in each of your fields with at least 3 grain (and no vegetable). Place the vegetable below the grain.

A115.name: Chief Forester
A115.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you also get a "Sow" action for exactly 1 field.

A117.name: Wood Carrier
A117.text: When you play this card, you immediately receive 1 wood for each (major and minor) improvement in front of you.

A118.name: Treegardener
A118.text: In the field phase of each harvest, you receive 1 wood. Additionally, you can buy up to 2 additional wood for 1 food each.

A12.name: Fireplace
A12.reason: available as the built-in major improvement

A121.name: Clay Puncher
A121.text: When you play this card and each time after you use an "Occupation" or the "Take 1 Clay" accumulation space, you receive 1 clay.

A122.name: Pan Baker
A122.text: Each time you use the "Sow and/or Bake" action space, you also receive 2 clay and 1 wood. (1-5 players) Each time you build a room/renovate, but only once per room/action, you can replace exactly 2 clay or 2 stone with 1 wood.

A124.name: Knapper
A124.text: Each time before you use an action space card on round spaces 5 to 7, you receive 1 stone. (1-5 players) When you play this card, if you live in a clay house with exactly 2 rooms, you immediately get 3 clay, 2 reed, and 2 stone.

A126.name: Master Workman
A126.text: Each time before you use an action space card on round spaces 1/2/3/4, you receive 1 wood/clay/reed/stone.

A127.name: Lodger
A127.text: This card provides room for 1 person, but only until the returning home phase of round 9. If you have not moved the person elsewhere by then, remove it from play.

A128.name: Riparian Builder
A128.reason: grants a discounted room build reactively to another player's Reed Bank use; building a room (cell adjacency, house-type cost) is engine-internal logic not exposed to card hooks.
A128.text: Each time another player uses the "Take 1 Reed" accumulation space, you can build a room: if you build a clay/stone room, you receive a discount of 1 clay/2 stone.

A129.name: Swagman
A129.reason: requires placing on an already-occupied action space (guest-token style); not supported.
A129.text: Immediately after each time you use the "Build rooms and/or stables" or "Take 1 Grain" action space, you can use the respective other with the same person (even if it is occupied).

A130.name: Mummy's Boy
A130.reason: requires placing a later person on an action space your own earlier person already occupies; guest-token/occupied-space placement is not supported.
A130.text: Each time you place a person after your first two people, you can place one of them on the same action space your second person is on.

A131.name: Craft Teacher
A131.text: Each time after you build the major improvement Joinery, Pottery, and Basketmaker's Workshop', you can play up to 2 occupations without paying an occupation cost.

A132.name: Publican
A132.reason: 'each time another player sows' can't be observed: the sow hook fires only for the sower's own cards (fire_player), not broadcast.
A132.text: Each time before another player sows, if you have at least 1 grain in your supply, you may give that player 1 grain, receiving 1 bonus point for that.

A134.name: Full Farmer
A134.text: When you play this card, you immediately receive 1 wood and 1 clay. During the scoring, you receive 1 bonus point for each pasture holding the maximum number of animals.

A135.name: Animal Reeve
A135.text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 complete rounds left to play, you immediately get 1/2/3/4 wood. During the scoring, each player with 2+/3+/4+ animals of each type gets 1/3/5 bonus points.

A136.name: Drudgery Reeve
A136.text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 complete rounds left to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. During the scoring, each player with at least 1/2/3 building resources of each type in their supply receives 1/3/5 bonus points.

A137.name: Riverine Shepherd
A137.text: Each time you use the "Take 1 Sheep" or "Take 1 Reed" accumulation space, you can also take 1 good from the other accumulation space of the two, if possible. (3-5 players) Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space you can also pay 1 wood to get 1 food for each person you have, and 1 reed.

A139.name: Hollow Warden
A139.text: When you play this card, you immediately get a "Major Improvement" action to build a Fireplace. Each time you use the "Hollow" Accumulation space, you also get 1 Food.

A14.name: Cooking Hearth
A14.reason: available as the built-in major improvement

A140.name: Shovel Bearer
A140.text: Each time you use the "Take 1 Clay" or "Take 2 Clay" accumulation space, you also receive a number of food equal to the amount of clay on the other accumulation space of the two.

A141.name: Turnip Farmer
A141.reason: checks whether two spaces are occupied 'immediately before the returning home phase'; occupied_by is reset to None before any round-boundary hook fires, so that state can't be observed.
A141.text: Immediately before the returning home phase of each round, if both the "Day Laborer" and "Take 1 Grain" action spaces are occupied, you receive 1 vegetable.

A142.name: Cordmaker
A142.text: Each time any player (including you) takes at least 2 reed from the "Take 1 Reed" accumulation space, you can choose to take 1 grain or buy 1 vegetable for 2 food. (3-5 players) Every improvement, room, and renovation costs you 1 stone less.

A144.name: Sequestrator
A144.text: Place 3 reed and 4 clay on this card. The next player to have 3 pastures/5 field tiles receives the 3 reed/4 clay (not retroactively).

A145.name: Ropemaker
A145.text: At the end of each harvest, you receive 1 reed from the general supply.

A146.name: Storehouse Steward
A146.text: Each time you take exactly 2/3/4/5 food from a food accumulation space, you also receive 1 stone/reed/clay/wood. (If you take 6 or more food, you do not get a bonus good.) (3-5 players) Each time you use the "Sheep Market", "Pig Market", or "Cattle Market" accumulation space, you can buy 1 additional animal of the respective type for 1 food.

A148.name: Woolgrower
A148.text: This card can hold a number of sheep equal to the number of completed feeding phases.

A149.name: House Artist
A149.text: Each time you use the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you also receive a "Build Rooms" action during which each room costs you 1 less reed.

A15.name: Well
A15.reason: available as the built-in major improvement

A150.name: Stagehand
A150.text: Each time another player uses the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you can take your choice of a "Build Fences", "Build Stables", or "Build Rooms" action with costs.

A151.name: Minstrel
A151.reason: needs a 'returning home phase' hook to check which round 1-4 spaces are unoccupied; occupied_by is reset before any card hook fires at that point, and it would also grant a full alternate space-use action.
A151.text: At the start of each returning home phase, if only one action space card on round spaces 1-4 is unoccupied, you can use that action space.

A152.name: Night-School Student
A152.reason: needs a 'returning home phase' hook to detect whether any player used an Occupation space that round; occupied_by is reset beforehand and no per-round usage log is kept.
A152.text: In each returning home phase in which no player returns a person from an occupation space, you can play an occupation for an occupation cost of 1 food.

A153.name: Pig Owner
A153.text: The first time after you play this card that you have 5 wild boars on your farm, you immediately receive 3 bonus points.

A154.name: Paymaster
A154.text: Each time another player uses a food accumulation space, you can give them 1 grain from your supply to receive 1 bonus point.

A156.name: Buyer
A156.text: Each time another player uses a reed, stone, sheep, or wild boar accumulation space, you can pay them 1 food to get 1 good of the respective type from the general supply.

A157.name: Bohemian
A157.reason: needs a 'returning home phase' hook to check whether an Occupation space is unoccupied; occupied_by is reset before any round-boundary hook fires.
A157.text: At the start of each returning home phase, if at least one "Occupation" action space is unoccupied, you get 1 food.

A158.name: Culinary Artist
A158.text: Each time another player uses the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you can exchange your choice of 1 grain/sheep/vegetable for 4/5/7 food.

A159.name: Joiner of the Sea
A159.text: Each time another player uses the "Fishing"/"Take 1 Reed" accumulation space, you can give them 1 wood to receive 2/3 food from the general supply. (4-5 players) Each time another player uses the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you get 1 food and 1 wood. Immediately after, you can buy exactly 1 vegetable for 2 food.

A16.name: Clay Oven
A16.reason: available as the built-in major improvement

A161.name: Patch Caretaker
A161.text: Each time you use an accumulation space, if you already used another accumulation space for the same type of good that work phase, you also receive 1 vegetable.

A162.name: Forest Tallyman
A162.reason: 'the gap between two occupied spaces' is a phantom action space that must consume a person placement; the engine has no mechanism for a dynamic extra placement slot, and a free card_action would misrepresent the worker cost.
A162.text: Each time both the "Take 3 Wood" and the "Take 1 Clay" action spaces are occupied, you can use the gap between them as an action space to receive 2 clay and 3 wood.

A163.name: Building Expert
A163.text: Each time you use the "Take Reed Stone Food (or Wood)" action space with the 1st/2nd/3rd/4th/5th person you place, you also get 1 wood/clay/reed/stone/stone.

A164.name: Wood Worker
A164.text: Each time you take wood from an accumulation space, you can exchange 1 wood for 1 sheep. Place the wood on the accumulation space.

A166.name: Haydryer
A166.text: Immediately before each harvest, you can buy 1 cattle for 4 food minus 1 food for each pasture you have. (The minimum cost is 0.)

A167.name: Breeder Buyer
A167.text: Each time you build at least 1 wood/clay/stone room and at least 1 stable on the same turn, you also get 1 sheep/wild boar/cattle.

A168.name: Animal Teacher
A168.text: Immediately after each time you use an "Occupation" action space, you can also buy 1 sheep/wild boar/cattle for 0/1/2 food.

A17.name: Stone Oven
A17.reason: available as the built-in major improvement

A18.name: Joinery
A18.reason: available as the built-in major improvement

A19.name: Pottery
A19.reason: available as the built-in major improvement

A20.name: Basketmaker's Workshop
A20.reason: available as the built-in major improvement
A20.text: If you have both copies of a guild, each harvest, you can turn 2 resources into food. to 6 bonus points (but not double-count resources.) During scoring, you may earn up

A5.name: Well
A5.reason: available as the built-in major improvement
A5.text: Place 1 food from the general supply on each of the next 5 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

A6.name: Clay Oven
A6.reason: available as the built-in major improvement
A6.text: Whenever you use a bread baking action, you can turn exactly 1 grain into 5 food. When you take this card, you can also bake bread immediately.

A7.name: Stone Oven
A7.reason: available as the built-in major improvement
A7.text: Whenever youuse a bread baking action, you can turn up to 2 grain into 4 food each. When you take this card, you can bake bread immediately.

A8.name: Joinery
A8.reason: available as the built-in major improvement
A8.text: In each harvest, you can use the Joinery to convert exactly 1 wood to 2 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 3/5/7 wood.

A9.name: Pottery
A9.reason: available as the built-in major improvement
A9.text: In each harvest, you can use the Pottery to convert exactly 1 clay to 2 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 3/5/7 clay.

B001.name: Upscale Lifestyle
B001.text: You immediately get 5 clay and a "Renovation" action. If you take the action, you must pay the renovation cost. (Cost 2F.) Immediately fence a farmyard space, without paying wood for the fences. (If you already have pastures, the new one must be adjacent to an existing one.)

B003.name: Moonshine
B003.text: Randomly select an occupation in your hand. Either play it for an occupation cost of 2 food, or give it to the player to your left.

B004.name: Wood Pile
B004.text: You immediately get a number of wood equal to the number of people you have on accumulation spaces.

B005.name: Store of Experience
B005.text: If you have 0-4/5/6/7 occupations left in hand, you immediately get 1 stone/reed/clay/wood.

B006.name: Excursion to the Quarry
B006.text: You immediately get a number of stone equal to the number of people you have.

B007.name: Wage
B007.reason: requires major-improvement board-row ('bottom row') metadata, which the engine's MAJOR_IMPROVEMENTS table doesn't track
B007.text: You immediately get 2 food and 1 additional food for each major improvement you have from the bottom row of the supply board.

B009.name: Beating Rod
B009.text: You can immediately choose to either get 1 reed or exchange 1 reed for 1 cattle. (Cost 3W 3F.) This card provides room for 1 person.

B011.name: Feedyard
B011.text: This card can hold 1 animal for each pasture you have, even different types. After the breeding phase of each harvest, you receive 1 food for each unused spot on this card.

B012.name: Stockyard
B012.reason: requires card-based animal storage capacity outside house/pastures; accommodation only consults house_capacity and pasture_capacity_bonus
B012.text: This card can hold up to 3 animals of the same type. (It is not considered a pasture.) (Cost 1W 1S.) Wooden rooms only cost you 2 wood and 2 reed each.

B014.name: Hawktower
B014.text: Place a stone room on round space 12. If you live in a stone house at the start of that round, you can build the stone room at no cost. Otherwise, discard the stone room.

B015.name: Carpenter's Bench
B015.reason: requires restricting a pasture build to only the specific wood taken this turn and to exactly one pasture; no such gating hook exists
B015.text: Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can use the taken wood (and only that) to build exactly 1 pasture. If you do, one of the fences is free.

B017.name: Forest Plow
B017.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can pay 2 wood to plow 1 field. Place the paid wood on the accumulation space (for the next visitor).

B018.name: Grassland Harrow
B018.prereq_text: 2 occupations and 1 building resource in your supply
B018.text: Add 1 to the current round for each building resource in your supply and place 1 field on the corresponding round space. At the start of that round, you can plow the field. (Cost 2W. Req 1 occ.) Place 2 field tiles on this card. Twice this game, when you use the"Farmland" action space, you can also plow 1 field from this card.

B020.name: Chain Float
B020.text: Add 7, 8, and 9 to the current round and place 1 field on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can plow the field.

B021.name: Hayloft Barn
B021.text: Place 4 food on this card. Each time you obtain at least 1 grain, you also get 1 food from this card. Once it is empty, you get a "Family Growth Even without Room" action.

B022.name: Walking Boots
B022.reason: requires placing an extra temporary family member mid-game and removing them later — the guest-token/extra-people mechanic the guide marks unsupported
B022.text: You immediately get 2 food. You must immediately place a person from your supply. If you do, in the next returning home phase, you must remove that person from play.

B023.name: Final Scenario
B023.text: Place the action space card for round 14 face up in front of you. Only you can use it until it is placed on the game board. (Cost 1R.) You can place exactly two people immediately after one another if at least one of them uses the "Sheep Market", "Pig Market", or "Cattle Market" accumulation space. (Cost 1W.) When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food. For each occupation you play, you get an additional "Bake Bread" action.

B026.name: Agrarian Fences
B026.reason: requires substituting Build Fences for one of Grain Utilization's own sub-actions. sub_actions.build_fences can perform the fence build itself now, but nothing exposes a hook to inject an alternate action CHOICE into an existing action space's own dispatch (_resolve_space's grain_utilization branch is a fixed sow/bake pair in engine.py); unlike bake_on_spaces (hard-wired specifically for adding Bake Bread to OTHER spaces), there's no analogous mechanism for adding Build Fences as a substitute action here.
B026.text: Each time you use the "Sow and/or Bake Bread" action space, you can take a "Build Fences" action instead of one of the two actions provided by the action space.

B027.name: Toolbox
B027.text: In the work phase, after each turn in which you build at least 1 room, stable, or fence, you can build the "Joinery", "Pottery", or "Basketmaker's Workshop" major improvement.

B028.name: Forestry Studies
B028.text: Each time after you use the "Take 3 Wood" accumulation space, you can return 2 wood to that space to play 1 occupation without paying an occupation cost.

B029.name: Cookery Lesson
B029.reason: requires detecting 'use a Cooking improvement' as a discrete same-turn event; cook conversions are a static rate query (raw_values/cook), never a fired event
B029.text: Each time you use an "Occupation" action space and a Cooking improvement on the same turn, you get 1 bonus point.

B030.name: Wood Palisades
B030.text: Instead of a fence piece, you can place 2 wood from your supply on fence spaces at the edge of your farmyard. These fence spaces with 2 wood are worth 1 bonus point.

B031.name: Pottery Yard
B031.text: During scoring, if there at least 2 orthogonally adjacent unused spaces in your farmyard, you get 2 bonus points. (You still get the negative points for those unused spaces.)

B032.name: Kettle
B032.text: At any time, you can exchange 1/3/5 grain for 3/4/5 food plus 0/1/2 bonus points.

B034.name: Special Food
B034.reason: requires detecting that animals taken from a space were fully accommodated (not discarded); no post-accommodation hook exists
B034.text: The next time you take animals from an accumulation space and accommodate all of them on your farm, you get 1 bonus point for each of these animals.

B035.name: Hook Knife
B035.reason: requires continuously monitoring sheep count against a once-per-game threshold; sheep can change during the unhooked breeding phase, so the threshold can't reliably be checked
B035.text: Once this game, when you have 9/8/7/6/5/5 sheep on your farm in a 1/2/3/4/5/6 player game, you immediately get 2 bonus points. (4VP. See below for cost.) For each person you have, you must pay an additional 1 clay and 1 food to play this card.

B037.name: Grange
B037.prereq_text: 6 field tiles and all animal types
B037.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food.

B038.name: Future Building Site
B038.reason: requires restricting which of the player's own farmyard cells are legal build/plow targets; no such legality hook exists (and this isn't a cost_mod)
B038.text: Until all other farmyard spaces are used, you cannot use spaces that are orthogonally adjacent to your house.

B040.name: Brewery Pond
B040.text: Each time you use the "Fishing" or "Take 1 Reed" accumulation space, you also get 1 grain and 1 wood.

B041.name: Hauberg
B041.text: Alternate placing 2 wood and 1 wild boar on the next 4 round spaces. You decide what to start with. At the start of these rounds, you get the goods.

B042.name: Forest Inn
B042.text: This is an action space for all. A player who uses it can exchange 5/7/9 wood for 8 wood and 2/4/7 food. When another player uses it, they must first pay you 1 food.

B043.name: Chophouse
B043.text: Each time you use the "Take 1 Grain/Vegetable" action space, place 1 food on each of the next 3/2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

B044.name: Chick Stable
B044.text: Add 3 and 4 to the current round and place 2 food on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (2VP. Cost 1W. Req 2 vegetable fields.) Place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

B046.name: Club House
B046.text: Place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces and 1 stone on the round space after that. At the start of these rounds, you get the respective good. (Cost 1C.) Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

B048.name: Forest Stone
B048.text: Place 2 food on this card. Each time you use a wood accumulation space, move 1 of these food to your supply. Each time you use a stone accumulation space, add 2 food to this card.

B049.name: Scales
B049.text: Each time after you place an improvement or occupation in front of you, if you have the same number of improvements and occupations in play, you get 2 food.

B051.name: Digging Spate
B051.text: Each time you use a clay accumulation space, you also get a number of food equal to the number of wild boar in your farmyard.

B052.name: Growing Farm
B052.prereq_text: at least as many pasture spaces as completed rounds
B052.text: You can only play this card if you have at least as many pasture spaces as the number of completed rounds. If you do, you get a number of food equal to the current round.

B053.name: Sculpture Course
B053.text: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can use this card to exchange your choice of 1 wood for 2 food, or 1 stone for 4 food.

B054.name: Tumbrel
B054.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 food. Each time after you take a "Sow" action, you get 1 food for each stable you have.

B055.name: Maintenance Premium
B055.text: Place 3 food on this card. Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get 1 food from this card. Each time you renovate, restock this card to 3 food. (Req 1 of your people on "Fishing".) Each time you use one of the four action spaces above the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get 1 additional food. (Cost 1W 1C.) At the start of each round, if you live in a wooden house, you get 1 food.

B058.name: Crack Weeder
B058.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food. For each vegetable you take from a field in the field phase of a harvest, you also get 1 food.

B059.name: Food Chest
B059.reason: requires knowing which action space a minor improvement was played from; the play hook's ctx carries no space_id and engine.py can't be edited to add it
B059.text: If you play this card on the "Major Improvement" action space, you immediately get 4 food. Otherwise, you get only 2 food.

B060.name: Brewing Water
B060.text: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you can pay 1 grain to place 1 food on each of the next 6 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

B063.name: Tasting
B063.text: Each time you use an "Occupation" action space, before paying the occupation cost, you can exchange 1 grain for 4 food.

B064.name: Mill Wheel
B064.text: Each time you use the "Sow and/or Bake Bread" action space while the "Fishing" accumulation space is occupied, you get an additional 2 food.

B065.name: Grain Depot
B065.reason: the reward tier depends on which resource the player chose to pay the cost in; the engine's cost model is a single fixed dict, not a player-chosen-material-with-differing-effect cost
B065.text: If you paid wood/clay/stone for this card, place 1 grain on each of the next 2/3/4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the grain. (Req 2 occ. Cost 2W.) Place 1 grain each on the remaining spaces for rounds 5, 8, 11, and 14. At the start of these rounds, you get the grain.

B067.name: Hand Truck
B067.text: Each time before you take a "Bake Bread" action, you also get 1 grain for each of your people occupying an accumulation space.

B069.name: Potters Market
B069.text: At any time, you can pay 3 clay and 2 food. If you do, place 1 vegetable on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the vegetable.

B070.name: New Purchase
B070.text: Before the start of each round that ends with a harvest, you can buy one of each of the following crops: 1 grain for 2 food 1 vegetable for 4 food

B071.name: Harvest House
B071.text: When you play this card, if the number of completed harvests is equal to the number of occupations you played, you immediately get 1 food, 1 grain and 1 vegetable.

B072.name: Love for Agriculture
B072.reason: requires letting 1-2-space pastures also act as sowable fields with reduced capacity; needs changes to sow validation and pasture capacity in the engine
B072.text: You can sow crops in pastures covering 1 or 2 farmyard spaces. If you do, these pastures are also considered fields and hold 1 and 2 animals less, respectively.

B073.name: Gift Basket
B073.text: When you play this card, if you have exactly 2/3/4/5 rooms, you immediately get 1 vegetable/food/grain/vegetable. (Req 5 occ in your supply.) Place 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.

B075.name: Wood Workshop
B075.text: Each time before you play or build an improvement, you get 1 wood.

B076.name: Ceilings
B076.text: Place 1 wood on next 5 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood. Remove the wood promised by this card from future round spaces the next time you renovate. (1VP. Req 3 occ. Cost 1F.) Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you also get 3 clay.

B078.name: Reed Belt
B078.text: Place 1 reed on each remaining space for rounds 5, 8, 10, and 12. At the start of these rounds, you get the reed.

B079.name: Corf
B079.text: Each time any player (including you) takes at least 3 stone from an accumulation space, you get 1 stone from the general supply. (Cost 1C.) At any time, you can exchange 2/3/4 clay for 1/2/3 stone.

B081.name: Handcart
B081.text: Before the start of each work phase, you can take 1 building resource from at most one wood/clay/reed/stone accumulation space containing at least 6/5/4/4 building resources of the same type.

B082.name: Value Assets
B082.text: After each harvest, you can buy exactly one of the following building resources: 1 wood for 1 food 1 clay for 1 food 1 reed for 2 food 1 stone for 2 food

B083.name: Reed Belt
B083.text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 wild boar, 1 food, 1 cattle, 1 food, and 1 sheep on this card. At any time, you can pay 1 clay to take the top good. (Req 3 occ. Cost 1R.) Place 1 wild boar on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wild boar.

B085.name: Farm Hand
B085.reason: requires a stable in the conceptual 'center' of a 2x2 field block; the 15-cell farmyard grid has no such position.
B085.text: Once you have 4 field tiles arranged in a 2x2, you can use a "Build Stables" action to build a stable in the center of the 2x2. This stable provides room for a person but no animal.

B086.name: Truffle Searcher
B086.reason: DB text corrupted by a parse error; the card-storage mechanism ('holds N wild boar') has no specified acquisition/release trigger once the contaminating text is removed.
B086.text: This card can hold a number of wild boar equal to the number of completed feeding phases. (1-5 players) Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you can also either build exactly 1 room or renovate your house. Either way, you have to pay the cost.

B088.name: Established Person
B088.text: If your house has exactly 2 rooms, immediately renovate it without paying any building resources. If you do, you can immediately afterward take a "Build Fences" action.

B090.name: Cooperative Plower
B090.text: Each time you use the "Plow 1 field" action space while the "Take 1 Grain" action space is occupied, you can plow 1 additional field.

B092.name: Little Stick Knitter
B092.text: From Round 5 on, each time you use the "Take 1 Sheep" accumulation space, you can also take a "Family Growth with Room Only" action.

B093.name: Little Stick Knitter
B093.text: Place 1 food from your supply on each of the next 2, 3, or 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food back and your choice of a "Sow" or "Build Fences" action.

B094.name: Stock Protector
B094.reason: grants an extra worker placement after Fencing; the engine's only double-placement mechanism (Lasso) is hardcoded to the animal markets.
B094.text: Each time before you use the "Fencing" action space, you get 2 wood. Immediately after that "Fencing" aciton, you can place another person. (1-5 players) Each time you build a major improvement, reduce the stone cost by the number of rooms you have built onto your initial house.

B096.name: Tree Farm Joiner
B096.text: Place 1 wood on each of the next 2 odd-numbered round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood and, immediately afterward, a "Minor Improvement" action.

B100.name: Clutterer
B100.text: During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each card played after this one that has "accumulation space(s)" in its text.

B101.name: Furniture Carpenter
B101.text: Each harvest, if any player (including you) owns the Joinery or an upgrade thereof, you can buy exactly 1 bonus point for 2 food.

B103.name: Field Merchant
B103.reason: requires a 'decline the Major/Minor Improvement action' outcome; the major_improvement space always requires building an improvement or playing a minor (no pass/decline option exists).
B103.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood and 1 reed. Each time you decline a "Minor/Major Improvement" action, you get 1 food/vegetable instead.

B105.name: Case Builder
B105.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 good of each of the following types, if you have at least 2 of that good in your supply already: food, grain, vegetable, reed, wood.

B106.name: Moral Crusader
B106.text: Immediately before the start of each round, if there are goods on remaining round spaces that are promised to you, you get 1 food.

B110.name: Pavior
B110.text: At the end of each preparation phase, if you have at least 1 stone in your supply, you get 1 food. In round 14, you get 1 vegetable instead.

B111.name: Rustic
B111.text: For each clay room you build, you get 2 food and 1 bonus point. (This does not apply to stone rooms and renovated wood rooms.)

B112.name: Silokeeper
B112.text: Each time you use the action space card that has been revealed right before the most recent harvest, you also get 1 grain.

B113.name: Patch Caregiver
B113.text: When you play this card, you can choose to buy 1 grain for 1 food, or 1 vegetable for 3 food. This card is a field.

B115.name: Tinsmith Master
B115.text: You can hold 1 additional animal in each pasture without a stable. Each time you sow in a field, you can place 1 additional crop of the respective type in that field.

B116.name: Shoreforester
B116.reason: requires reacting to the preparation-phase accumulation-space replenishment (reed_bank going from empty to 1 reed); no hook fires at replenish time.
B116.text: When you play this card and each time 1 reed is placed on an empty "Take 1 Reed" accumulation space in the preparation phase, you get 1 wood.

B117.name: Informant
B117.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood. After each work phase, if you have more stone than clay in your supply, you get 1 wood.

B119.name: Lumberjack
B119.text: You immediately get 1 wood. Additionally, place 1 wood on each of the next round spaces, up to the number of fences you built. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood. ˇ ⇒ Fences placed e.g. with Hops Field C18 or Fence Builder I263 do not count as fences builded.

B120.name: Sweep
B120.text: Each time before you use the action space card left of the card that has been most recently placed on a round space, you get 2 clay.

B122.name: Mineralogist
B122.text: Each time you use a clay/stone accumulation space, you also get 1 of the other good, stone/clay.

B124.name: Trimmer
B124.text: In each work phase, after you enclose at least one farmyard space, you get 2 stone. (Subdividing an exisiting pasture does not count.)

B125.name: Estate Worker
B125.text: Place 1 wood, 1 clay, 1 reed, and 1 stone in this order on the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the respective building resource.

B127.name: Seducer
B127.text: When you play this card in Round 5 or later, you can immediately pay 1 stone, 1 grain, 1 vegetable, and 1 sheep to take a "Family Growth Even without Room" action.

B128.name: Plumber
B128.text: Each time after you use the "Major Improvement" action space, you can take a "Renovation" action, paying 2 clay or 2 stone less for the renovation.

B129.name: Seatmate
B129.reason: requires using an action space already occupied by other players; placing on occupied spaces is explicitly unsupported.
B129.text: You can use the action space on round space 13 even if it is occupied by one or more people of the players to your immediate left and right.

B130.name: Full Peasant
B130.reason: grants a full bonus use of Sow/Bake Bread or Fencing with player-chosen targets; no channel carries those open-ended parameters (fence edges, per-field sow lists) outside the normal placement flow.
B130.text: Each time after you use the "Sow and/or Bake Bread" or "Fencing" action space while the other is unoccupied, you can pay 1 food to use that other space with the same person.

B131.name: Equipper
B131.text: Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can play a minor improvement.

B132.name: Estate Master
B132.text: Once you have no unused farmyard spaces left, you get 1 bonus point for each vegetable that you harvest.

B133.name: Village Peasant
B133.text: At the start of scoring, you get a number of vegetables equal to the smallest of the numbers of major improvements, minor improvements, and occupations you have.

B134.name: Housebook Master
B134.text: After playing this card, if you renovate to stone in round 13/12/11 or before, you immediately get 1/2/3 food and 1/2/3 bonus points.

B135.name: Nutrition Expert
B135.text: At the start of each round, you can exchange a set comprised of 1 animal of any type, 1 grain, and 1 vegetable for 5 food and 2 bonus points.

B137.name: Wholesaler
B137.text: Place 1 vegetable, 1 wild boar, 1 stone, and 1 cattle on this card. Each time you use an action space card on round spaces 8 to 11, you get the corresponding good from this card.

B138.name: Forest Guardian
B138.reason: requires forcing another player to pay you before they can take wood from an accumulation space (a legality-gating replacement effect on other players' actions); directly affecting other players' actions is unsupported.
B138.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 wood. Each time before another player takes at least 5 wood from an accumulation space, they must first pay you 1 food.

B139.name: Forest Scientist
B139.text: In the returning home phase of each round, if there is no wood left on the game board, you get 1 food - from round 5 on, even 2 food.

B140.name: Farmyard Worker
B140.reason: requires tracking 'placed a good on a farmyard space' across a whole work phase, including animal accommodation; no hook fires when animals are placed via the accommodate flow, so this could only be partially detected (sowing only), misrepresenting the card.
B140.text: At the end of each work phase in which you placed at least 1 good on 1 of your farmyard spaces, you get 2 food.

B141.name: Field Caretaker
B141.text: When you play this card, you can immediately exchange 0/1/3 clay for 1/2/3 grain. This card is a field.

B143.name: Clay Warden
B143.reason: DB text is internally contradictory ('you get 1 clay' immediately followed by 'you also get 1 clay/food' with an unclear player-count mapping); likely still parse-contaminated and not reliably implementable.
B143.text: Each time another player uses the "Take 1/2 Clay" accumulation space in a 3/4 player game, you get 1 clay. In a 3/4 player game, you also get 1 clay/food.

B144.name: Collier
B144.text: Each time after you use the "Take 1 Clay" accumulation space, you get 1 reed and 1 wood. With 3 or more players, you get 1 additional wood on the "Take 1/2/3 Clay" accumulation space in 3/4/5 player.

B146.name: Illusionist
B146.text: Each time you use a building resource accumulation space, you can discard exactly 1 card from your hand to get 1 additional building resource of the accumulating type.

B147.name: Huntsman
B147.text: Each time after you use a wood accumulation space, you can pay 1 grain to get 1 wild boar.

B148.name: Pet Broker
B148.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 sheep. You can keep 1 sheep on each occupation in front of you.

B149.name: Open Air Farmer
B149.reason: spends 3 of the player's 4 available stables permanently; MAX_STABLES is a fixed engine constant with no per-player reduction mechanism, so this cost can't be paid faithfully.
B149.text: When you play this card, remove exactly 3 stables in your supply from play to build a pasture covering 2 farmyard spaces. You only need to pay a total of 2 wood for the fences.

B150.name: Large-Scale Farmer
B150.reason: grants a full bonus use of Build Rooms/Stables or Major Improvement (cell/room/improvement/minor choice plus cost) after the other space is unoccupied; would require re-implementing large parts of the building/improvement rules outside the normal placement flow.
B150.text: Each time after you use the "Build Rooms and/or Build Stables" or "Major Improvement" action space while the other is unoccupied, you can pay 1 food to use that other space with the same person.

B151.name: Little Peasant
B151.reason: requires action spaces not being considered occupied for this player; placing on occupied spaces is explicitly unsupported.
B151.text: You immediately get 1 stone. As long as you live in a wooden house with exactly 2 rooms, action spaces - excluding "Starting Player" - are not considered occupied for you.

B152.name: Junior Artist
B152.text: Each time after you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you can pay 1 food to use an unoccupied "Traveling Players" or "Occupation" action space with the same person.

B153.name: Housemaster
B153.text: During scoring, total the point values of your major improvements. The smallest value counts double. If the total is at least 5/7/9/11, you get 1/2/3/4 bonus points.

B154.name: Sheep Keeper
B154.text: You can only play this card if you have less than 7 sheep. Once this game, when you have 7 sheep in your farmyard, you immediately get 3 bonus points and 2 food.

B155.name: Art Teacher
B155.reason: requires substituting food from the Traveling Players accumulation space as payment for occupation costs; the engine's occupation-cost payment has no hook allowing an alternate resource source.
B155.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood and 1 reed. Each time you pay an occupation cost, you can use food from the "Traveling Players" accumulation space. (4-5 players) Each time you use the "Resource Market" action space, you also get your choice of 1 clay or 1 grain.

B157.name: Salter
B157.text: At any time, you can pay 1 sheep/wild boar/cattle from your farmyard. If you do, place 1 food on each of the next 3/5/7 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

B158.name: District Manager
B158.text: At the end of each work phase, if you used both the "Take 3 Wood" and "Take 2/4 Wood" accumulation spaces, you get 5 food in 4/5 player game.

B159.name: Lieutenant General
B159.reason: requires reacting to another player's plow action, but the 'plow' event only fires to the acting player's own cards (to_all=False) -- no card can observe another player's plow.
B159.text: For each field tile that another player places next to an exisiting field tile, you get 1 food from the general supply. In round 14, you get 1 grain instead.

B160.name: Pub Owner
B160.text: Immediately, when you play this card, and at the end of each work phase, in which the "Take 3 Wood", "Take 1 Clay", and "Take 1 Reed" accumulation spaces are all occupied, you get 1 grain.

B161.name: Weakling
B161.text: Each time it is your turn in the work phase, if there are one or more accumulation spaces with 5+ goods on them and you do not use any of them, you get 1 vegetable.

B162.name: Forest Clearer
B162.text: Each time you obtain exactly 2/3/4 wood from a wood accumulation space, you get 1 additional wood and 1/0/1 food.

B165.name: Game Provider
B165.text: Immediately before each harvest, you can discard 1/3/4 grain from different fields to get 1/2/3 wild boars.

B167.name: Stable Sergeant
B167.text: When you play this card, you can pay 2 food to get 1 sheep, 1 wild boar, and 1 cattle, but only if you can accommodate all three animals on your farm.

B168.name: Pasture Master
B168.text: Each time you renovate, you get 2 food and 1 additional animal of the respective type in each of your pastures with stable.

BI01.name: Sparren Castle
BI01.text: Whenever anyone uses a Wood/Stone Accumulation space, place 1 Wood/Stone from the general supply on this card. Whenever you place a person on a "Renovation" Action space, take up to 5 resources from this card. (Before scoring, discard all the resources from this card.)

BI02.name: Bielefeld Cards
BI02.text: Whenever you use an Accumulation space, place all the items received on this card instead of placing them in your supply. Whenever there is 4 or more of the same item on this card, move exactly 4 of those to your supply. (Before scoring, discard the items on this card.)

BI04.name: Spielwiese
BI04.text: Reveal 2 Occupations randomly from the deck and add them to your hand. Look at the player size number on each card (either 1, 3, or 4). Take as many different Building resources as the smaller of the two numbers.

BI05.name: Home Zoo
BI05.text: This card can hold up to 1 Sheep, 1 Wild boar and 1 Cattle. You may only place animals here when you receive them from Accumulation spaces. While here, they do not participate in the Breeding phase of Harvest. During the last Harvest, you may exchange animals here for 1 Bonus point each.

BI06.name: Conspiracy
BI06.text: In the Field phase of each Harvest, you receive 1 Food for every 2 BI-Deck cards you have played. At the end of the game, every player receives 1/2/3 Bonus points for 1/3/5 played BI-Deck cards.

BI07.name: Baking Powder
BI07.text: Whenever you use the "Sow and/or Bake Bread" Action space, place 1 Grain from the supply on each of your Major Improvements. Whenever you play an Occupation, move these Grain to your supply. (Before scoring, discard the Grain from your Major Improvements.)

BI08.name: Custard
BI08.text: You immediately receive 1 Food for every Harvest remaining when you play this card. Whenever you take Stone from an Accumulation space leave 1 Stone on the space.

BI09.name: Alm
BI09.text: When you play this card, you may remove 1 stable from your farmyard and receive 1 Cattle. This card can hold an unlimited number of Cattle. (This card does not count as a pasture when scoring).

BI10.name: The 52nd Latitude
BI10.text: Immediately place 1 Stone from the general supply on 1 unused farmyard space. (It now counts as used.) At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each empty plowed field orthogonally adjacent to this Stone.

BI11.name: Schlachter Church
BI11.text: Whenever another player converts an animal to Food with a Cooking Improvement, place it on this card. At any time, you may convert all the animals on this card to 1 Food each. Afterward, this card loses its function but still counts as an Improvement.

BI12.name: Skybridge
BI12.text: Immediately place Stone from your supply between any adjacent rooms of your home. Between each 2 rooms you may place exactly 1 Stone. In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you receive 1 Grain for each placed Stone. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each placed Stone.

BI13.name: Wooden Buttons
BI13.text: Immediately take 1 Guest token for each empty room in your home. Place these Guests as you would additional Family members and return them at the end of this round. You do not have room for offspring this round.

BI14.name: Linen Weaver
BI14.text: At the start of each round, this Occupation is passed left to the next player. It only counts as a played Occupation for the player who has it. All their Improvement costs are reduced by 1 Wood. When the original player has it, their Improvement costs are reduced by 1 Clay, 1 Stone, and 1 Reed also.

BI15.name: Vampire
BI15.text: Play this card only if the player on your right just used an Accumulation space on the previous turn. You immediately use the same Action and receive the same amount of items. Take the items from the general supply.

BI16.name: Institute Founder
BI16.text: When you play this card, you may immediately pay 1 Food for 1 Grain. In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, your 4th and 5th Family members need only 1 Food.

BI17.name: White Brazilian
BI17.text: Play this card only if your top 5 farmyard spaces are unused. Place 1 stable and 1 Wild boar on the leftmost or rightmost farmyard space in this row. Flip a coin. If the coin lands heads, keep both the Wild boar and stable, otherwise keep only the stable.

BI18.name: Apple Man
BI18.text: Once during each Returning home phase, you may pay 1 Vegetable from your supply to take a "Plow 1 field", "Build fences", or "Build stables" action without placing a person.

BI19.name: Deaconess
BI19.text: As long as you have exactly 2 Family members, in the Feeding phase of Harvest 1/2/3/4/5/6, you receive 1/2/3/4/5/6 Food.

BI20.name: Survivalist
BI20.text: (Your Family will eat anything.) In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, the Survivalist can convert up to 1 of each Building material (Wood, Clay, Reed, and Stone) to 2 Food each. In each Harvest you use this, place 1 of these Food on the "Fishing" Action space.

BI21.name: Chief Editor
BI21.text: The next time you use the "Fishing" Action space with a person, you also receive 2 Bonus points. After that, you receive 1 Bonus point whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space with a person.

BI22.name: Protest Singer
BI22.text: (Your system has no limits.) You may keep 1 animal on each unused space on your farmyard. (The spaces still count as unused.) These animals do not participate in the Breeding phase of Harvest.

BI23.name: Systems Theorist
BI23.text: Whenever you build at least 1 stable, you receive 1 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 2/3/4 stables adjacent to each other.

BI24.name: Member of a Co-operative
BI24.text: Whenever another player performs a "Family growth" action, you may place 1 Food from the supply on this card. Once during a 3/4/5 player game, you may pay 3/4/5 Food from this card to play your next "Family growth" action as "Family growth even without room".

C001.name: Overhaul
C001.reason: razes and rebuilds existing fences with new pieces -- moving/removing built fences is unsupported
C001.text: Immediately raze all of your fences, add up to 3 fences from your supply, and rebuild them. (You do not lose any animals during this.)

C004.name: Writing Boards
C004.text: You immediately get 1 wood for each occupation you have in front of you.

C005.name: Remodeling
C005.text: You immediately get 1 clay for each clay room and for each major improvement you have. (Cost 1F.) Immediately place 1 stone on each of your empty fields. Harvest them during the next field phase. These fields are considered planted until then.

C007.name: Blade Shears
C007.text: You immediately get your choice of 3 food or 1 food for each sheep you have. (Keep the sheep.)

C008.name: Plant Fertilizer
C008.text: In each field with exactly 1 good, you can immediately place 1 additional good of the same type.

C009.name: Automatic Water Trough
C009.text: If you can accommodate the animal, you can immediately buy 1 sheep/wild boar/cattle for 0/1/2 food.

C010.name: Bunk Beds
C010.reason: conditional room-gated capacity (once you have 4 rooms, hold 5 people) and per-species card storage (1 sheep/1 boar/1 cattle) aren't expressible via the flat extra_rooms/house_capacity queries
C010.text: Once you have 4 rooms, your house can hold 5 people. (1VP. Cost 2W. Req 2 occ.) This card can hold up to 1 sheep, 1 wild boar, and 1 cattle.

C012.name: Cattle Farm
C012.text: For each pasture you have, you can keep 1 cattle on this card.

C013.name: Wood Slide Hammer
C013.reason: renovating directly from wood to stone, skipping the clay stage: sub_actions.renovate hardcodes RENOVATION_TARGET = {wood: clay, clay: stone} internally with no override/ctx channel to redirect a wood house straight to stone, and sub_actions.py can't be edited -- still not expressible.
C013.text: On your first renovation, if you have at least 5 wood rooms, you can renovate to stone directly and you get a discount of 2 stone on the renovation cost. (1VP. Req 3 grain fields.) You no longer need reed to renovate or build a room.

C015.name: Trellis
C015.text: Each time before you use the "Take 1 Wild Boar" accumulation space, you can take a "Build Fences" action. (You must pay wood for the fences as usual.) (Cost 2F.) You can immediately take a "Build Fences" action, during which you do not have to pay wood for fences that you build next to field tiles.

C017.name: Newly Plowed Field
C017.text: When you play this card, you can immediately plow 1 field, which needs not be adjacent to another field.

C018.name: Roll-Over Plow
C018.text: At any time, if you have at least 3 planted fields, you can discard all goods from one of those fields to plow 1 field. (Cost 3W. Req 3 occ.) Place 4 field tiles on this card. Each time you use the "Farmland" action space, you can also plow up to 2 fields from this card.

C020.name: Mole Plow
C020.text: Each time you use the "Plow 1 Field" or "Plow 1 Field and/or Sow" action space, you can plow 1 additional field.

C021.name: Heart of Stone
C021.reason: reacts to an action-space card being revealed -- no reveal hook exists (only round_start/space_used)
C021.text: Each time a Stone accumulation space is revealed, if you have room in your house, you can immediately take a "Family Growth" action without placing a person.

C022.name: Basket Chair
C022.reason: moves the first-placed person to this card and grants an extra person placement this turn -- same unsupported mechanic as guest tokens/extra placements
C022.text: When you play this card, you can immediately move the first person you placed this work phase to this card. If you do, immediately afterward, you can place another person.

C023.name: Job Contract
C023.reason: lets one person use two action spaces in sequence outside animal markets -- lasso is hardcoded to the animal-market double-placement case only
C023.text: If both are unoccupied, you can use the "Day Laborer" and the adjacent "Occupation" action space with a single person (in that order). Afterward, both spaces are considered occupied.

C024.name: Bed in the Grain Field
C024.reason: grants a free, scheduled Family Growth action (no person placed) at the next harvest -- no hook fires at 'start of harvest', and replicating family-growth bookkeeping (people_total/newborns/family_growth event) from a deck module isn't supported
C024.text: At the start of the next harvest, you get a "Family Growth" action if you have room for the newborn.

C028.name: Teacher's Desk
C028.text: Each time you use the "Major Improvement" or "Renovation and Improvement" action space, you can also play 1 occupation at an occupation cost of 1 food.

C029.name: Beer Table
C029.text: At the end of the field phase of each harvest, you can pay 1 grain from your supply to get 2 bonus points. If you do, all other players get 1 food each. (Cost 1W 1C 2S 1R.) During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each stone room you have. You can only use one card to get bonus points for your stone house.

C031.name: Writing Chamber
C031.reason: bonus equals the sum of the player's other negative score categories; computing that from within score_bonus risks recursing back into score_bonuses/score_player
C031.text: During scoring, you get a number of bonus points equal to the total of negative points you have, to a maximum of 7 bonus points.

C032.name: Abort Oriel
C032.prereq_text: no player has 5+ cards in front of them
C032.text: You can no longer play this card when any player (including you) has 5 or more cards in front of them.

C033.name: Greening Plan
C033.text: During scoring, if you then have at least 2/4/5/6 unplanted fields, you get 1/2/3/5 bonus points.

C034.name: Elephantgrass Plant
C034.text: Immediately after each harvest, you can use this card to exchange exactly 1 reed for 1 bonus point.

C036.name: Clay Deposit
C036.text: Immediately after each time you use a clay accumulation space, you can exchange 1 clay for 1 bonus point. If you do, place the clay on the accumulation space. (3VP. Cost 1F. Play in round 3 or before.) From now on, you must pay 1 food for each new field tile that you place in your farmyard.

C038.name: Christianity
C038.text: When you play this card, all other players get 1 food each.

C039.name: Studio Boat
C039.reason: in 1-3 player games this card becomes an entirely new personal action space (a 'Traveling Players' proxy) -- adding action spaces dynamically isn't supported
C039.text: Each time you use the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you also get 1 bonus point. In games with 1-3 players, this card is considered "Traveling Players" (same effect as "Fishing").

C040.name: Canvas Sack
C040.reason: cost is a player choice between two alternative payments (1 grain or 1 reed) with different resulting gains -- the engine's cost model is one fixed dict, no alternative-payment mechanism
C040.text: When play this card, paying grain/reed for it, you immediately get 1 vegetable/4 wood. (Cost 2W 2C.) After the feeding phase of each harvest, you can exchange exactly 1 food for 2 different building resources of your choice or 1 vegetable.

C043.name: Farm Building
C043.reason: no hook fires when a major improvement is built (only rooms_built/stable_built/fences_built/renovate exist for structures)
C043.text: Each time you build a major improvement, place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (1VP. Cost 2W/2C 1R.) Place 1 food on each of the next 8 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

C045.name: Stew
C045.text: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, also place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

C046.name: Mandoline
C046.text: Once per round, you can pay 1 vegetable to get 1 bonus point. If you do, place 1 food on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

C047.name: Garden Claw
C047.text: Place 1 food on each remaining round space, up to three times the number of planted fields you have. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

C048.name: Farmstead
C048.reason: detecting 'the first unused farmyard space used this turn' needs a turn-boundary hook; the existing per-structure hooks can co-occur within one turn (e.g. Farm Expansion), risking double-counting with no turn-level dedupe available
C048.text: After each turn in which you make at least one unused farmyard space used, you get 1 food.

C049.name: Beer Stall
C049.reason: conversion count depends on the number of empty, unfenced stables (dynamic per-instance state); conversions' per_harvest cap is a static integer fixed at registration
C049.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, for each empty unfenced stable you have, you can exchange 1 grain for 5 food.

C050.name: Stable Yard
C050.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food for each complete round left to play. At any time, you can exchange 1 sheep plus 1 wild boar for 1 cattle. (1VP. Cost 1R.) Each time another player uses the "Fishing" accumulation space, they must first pay you 1 food. Then, in the returning home phase of that round, place 2 food on "Fishing".

C052.name: Huntsman's Hat
C052.text: For each new wild boar you get from the effect of an action space, you also get 1 food.

C053.name: Gypsy's Crock
C053.reason: reacts to a feeding-phase conversion of 2 goods at once -- the feeding action's conversion loop never fires a hook event
C053.text: Each time you use a cooking improvement to turn 2 goods into food at the same time, you get 1 additional food.

C054.name: Market Stall
C054.reason: cost and effect both spend structural components (a stable, a fence, from supply) rather than resource goods -- the pay/cost system only handles the resources dict
C054.text: Remove 1 stable from your supply. After the field phase of each harvest, you can exchange 1 grain plus 1 fence (both from your supply) for 5 food.

C055.name: Studio
C055.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 wood/clay/stone into 2/2/3 food. (Cost 1W.) For each new stable you build, you get 1 food - for your last one, get 3 food. Each time you build stables, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 clay instead of wood.

C057.name: Crudite
C057.text: When you play this card, you can immediately buy exactly 1 vegetable for 3 food. At any time, you can discard 1 vegetable on top of another vegetable in a field to get 4 food.

C058.name: Woodcraft
C058.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, if immediately afterward you have at most 5 wood in your supply, you get 1 food. (2VP. Cost 1S 1 vegetable.) In each feeding phase, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 vegetable into 5 food. During scoring, you get 1 bonus point each for your 5th and 6th vegetable.

C060.name: Small Potter's Oven
C060.reason: requires returning (removing) an already-built major improvement -- no removal mechanism for built majors exists
C060.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 5 food. Each time before you get a "Bake Bread" action, you can build the "Clay Oven" or "Stone Oven" major improvement.

C061.name: Beer Stein
C061.text: Each time you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can use this card once to turn 1 grain into 2 food and 1 bonus point.

C062.name: Cooking Hearth Extension
C062.reason: doubles each owned cooking improvement's rate once per harvest -- needs per-major usage tracking and a dynamic rate override beyond the conversions/cook query shapes
C062.text: Each harvest, you can use each of your cooking improvements once to get double the amount of food for 1 animal or vegetable.

C063.name: Craft Brewery
