I240.name: Cowherd
I240.text: Whenever you use the "1 Cattle" action space, you receive 1 additional cattle from the general supply.

I241.name: Clay Plasterer
I241.text: Renovating your wooden hut to a clay hut costs you only 1 clay and 1 reeds. Each room of your clay hut costs you 3 clay and 2 reeds.

I242.name: Clay Hut Builder
I242.text: Once you live in a clay hut, place 2 clay on each of the next 5 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the clay.

I243.name: Bricklayer
I243.text: Pay 1 less clay for each improvement and renovation. Pay 2 less clay for each room.

I244.name: Layabout
I244.reason: suppresses the field, breeding, AND feeding phases for one player for one whole harvest; those phases are unconditional per-player loops in _start_harvest/feeding with no per-player skip flag or hook -- there is nothing for a card to intercept.
I244.text: Once you have played this card, you may not take part in the next harvest.

I245.name: Market Crier
I245.text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" action space, you can take an additional 1 grain and 1 vegetable. If you do this, the other players each receive 1 grain from the general supply.

I246.name: Milking Hand
I246.text: In the field phase of the harvest, you receive food if you have cattle, without having to give up the cattle: for 5 or more cattle, 3 food; for 3 or 4 cattle, 2 food; for 1 cattle, 1 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for every 2 cattle.

I247.name: Butcher
I247.text: You can convert your animals into food at any time. You receive 1 food for each sheep, 2 for each wild boar and 3 for each cattle.

I248.name: Net Fisherman
I248.reason: requires a distinct 'returning home' checkpoint between the work phase and the next round's preparation phase to claim a space's leftover food ahead of other players; no such hook exists, and by the time round_start fires, the Fishing space's supply has already been re-accumulated for the new round, corrupting the amount being claimed.
I248.text: If one of your people uses an action space that directly provides reeds, you can take all food from the "Fishing" action space in the returning home phase (phase 4).

I249.name: Puppeteer
I249.reason: paying 1 food to play an occupation (without its own play cost) off another player's action would require duplicating _play_occupation's internals (cost, hand removal, occs_played bookkeeping, occupation_played firing) inside a card hook; the identically-named Puppeteer in deck C (C152) declined this same duplication per the architecture's engine-extension-over-duplication guidance.
I249.text: Whenever another player chooses the "Traveling players" action on an action space, you can pay 1 food to play an occupation.

I250.name: Sheep Whisperer
I250.text: Add 4, 7, 9 and 11 to the number of the current round and place 1 sheep on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the sheep.

I251.name: Reed Buyer
I251.text: Whenever reed is taken for the first time in a round, you may give the player who takes it 1 food in exchange for 1 of the reed. The other player receives an additional 1 food from the general supply as compensation.

I252.name: Pig Breeder
I252.text: Your wild boar breed at the end of round 12, if there is room for the piglet. When you play this card, you receive 1 wild boar.

I253.name: Pig Catcher
I253.text: Whenever you use a person to take wood that is on an action space, you can leave 2 of that wood on the space and receive a wild boar instead.

I254.name: Groom
I254.text: Once you have a stone house, you can build 1 stable at the beginning of each round at a cost of 1 wood. You do not need to place a family member on an action space to do this.

I255.name: Stone Buyer
I255.text: Whenever stone is taken for the first time in a round, you may give the player who takes it 1 food in exchange for 1 of the stone. The other player receives an additional 1 food from the general supply.

I256.name: Stone Carver
I256.text: In each harvest, the Stone Carver can convert up to 1 stone to 3 food.

I257.name: Street Musician
I257.text: You receive 1 grain whenever another player takes the "Traveling Players" action on an action space.

I258.name: Cabinetmaker
I258.text: In each harvest, the Cabinetmaker can convert up to 1 wood to 2 food.

I259.name: Animal Dealer
I259.text: Whenever you use one of the "1 Sheep", "1 Wild Boar" or "1 Cattle" action spaces, you can pay 1 food to take 1 additional animal of that type.

I260.name: Taster
I260.text: Whenever another player is the starting player, you can pay them 1 food at the start of the round and be the first to place a family member. After that, play starts with the starting player as usual.

I261.name: Outrider
I261.text: Whenever you use the most recent round card with one of your people, you receive 1 additional grain.

I262.name: Water Carrier
I262.text: Once any player has built the Well A10 , place 1 food on the remaining round spaces. At the start of these Rounds, you receive the food.

I263.name: Fence Builder
I263.reason: reassessed against sub_actions.build_fences: the follow-up fence build itself (with player-chosen edges) is expressible now (space_used hook on a marked space + card_action, params channel for the edges). Still gated on a different, unfixed piece: placing the marker spends one of the player's 15 physical fence pieces ('from now on, you have only 14 fences available for building') PERMANENTLY, including for their ordinary Fencing-space builds -- but MAX_FENCES is a hardcoded global constant state.py's can_build_fences/build_fences check directly, with no per-player cap-reduction hook (cost_mod only adjusts PRICE, never the 15-fence count). Registering the marker+bonus-build half while silently dropping the '-1 max fences' consequence would make the card strictly better than printed (a free extra fence-build ability with no offsetting cost), which is the wrong direction to approximate in --unlike e.g. Carpenter's Axe's omitted clause, which only ever makes a card weaker than printed -- so it stays fully unimplemented rather than half-registered.
I263.text: When you play this card, place one of your fences on an action space of your choice. If you use an action on this action space, you can also build fences as an additional action.

I264.name: Fencer
I264.text: Whenever another player builds 1 to 4 fences, you receive 1 wood from the general supply. Whenever another player builds 5 or more fences, you receive 2 wood.

I265.name: Fence Deliveryman
I265.reason: pre-places unbuilt fences on future round spaces and lets the player build them for food instead of wood, but which of up to 15 edges to build is an open-ended choice with no channel outside the normal Build Fences flow (same gap as B088/B093/B130/I263), and it would have to be resolved from a round_start hook, where GUIDE.md disallows prompting.
I265.text: Add 6 and 10 to the number of the current round. Place 4 of your fences on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 2 food to build all 4 fences immediately.

I337.name: Clay Deposit
I337.text: This card is an additional action space. A player who uses this action space must pay you 1 food and receives 5 clay. If you use the Clay Deposit yourself, you may choose to take 2 bonus points instead of the clay.

I340.name: Rancher
I340.text: Whenever, at the start of a round, you are using more farmyard spaces than every other player, you receive 1 wood.

I63.name: Moldboard Plough
I63.text: Twice during the game, when you use the "Plough 1 Field" action space, you may plough 2 fields instead of 1.

I64.name: Alms
I64.text: When you play this card, you receive 1 food for each completed round of the game. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

I65.name: Baker's Kitchen
I65.prereq_text: an oven built (Clay Oven or Stone Oven)
I65.text: Whenever you use a bread baking action, you can use the Baker's Kitchen to convert up to 2 grain into 5 food each. When you play this card, you can also bake bread immediately .

I66.name: Village Well
I66.text: Place 1 food each on the next 3 remaining round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

I67.name: Threshing Board
I67.text: Whenever you use the "Plough 1 Field" or "Plough Field and Sow" action spaces, you can additionally bake bread.

I68.name: Harrow
I68.reason: grants a 'plough 2 fields instead of 1' option to OTHER players (who must pay the owner 2 food to use it); the choice dispatch (_apply_choice) requires the card instance to be in the resolving player's OWN in_play list, so a non-owner can never be prompted to invoke someone else's card -- there is no channel for 'another player may optionally activate your card for a fee'.
I68.text: Once during the game, when you use either the "Plough 1 Field" or the "Plough Field and Sow" action space, you can plough 2 fields instead of 1. Each other player can also do this once during the game, but must pay you 2 food to do it.

I69.name: Strawberry Patch
I69.text: Place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

I70.name: Punner
I70.reason: requires reacting to another player's plough action (via the Harrow or a plough card), but the 'plow' event only fires to the acting player's own cards (fire_player, to_all=False) -- no card can observe another player's plow (same gap as the B159 precedent).
I70.text: Whenever another player uses the Harrow I68 or a plough, you can immediately plough 1 field as well.

I71.name: Holiday House
I71.prereq_text: play this card at the latest during round 13
I71.text: In round 14, you cannot place any people. Play this card at the latest during round 13.

I72.name: Goose Pond
I72.text: Place 1 food each on the next 4 remaining round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

I73.name: Guest
I73.reason: guest tokens / extra people placements are explicitly unsupported.
I73.text: When you play this card, you receive a guest token, which you may place once in the next round as you would a person. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

I74.name: Grain Cart
I74.text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" action space, you receive 2 additional grain.

I75.name: Hand Mill
I75.text: During the feeding phase of the harvest, you can use the Hand Mill to turn either 1 grain into 2 food or 2 grain into 4 food.

I76.name: Rake
I76.text: At the end of the game, you receive 2 bonus points if you have at least 5 fields. If you have played the Harrow I68 , Punner I70 , Yoke K124 or a plough, you need to have at least 6 fields.

I77.name: Shepherd's Crook
I77.text: Whenever you fence a new pasture that covers at least 4 farmyard spaces, you receive 2 sheep and place them in the new pasture.

I78.name: Copse
I78.reason: requires sowing wood (not grain/vegetable) onto a card-held field with its own stacking rule; _do_sow raises for any crop type other than grain/vegetable, and the field crop-count formula (3 grain / 2 vegetable) has no analog for wood stacks.
I78.text: When you sow, you can plant wood on this card. There can be up to 2 stacks of wood on this card, as shown. The wood is treated the same as sown grain and is harvested during the field phase.

I79.name: Wood Cart
I79.text: Whenever you use a person to take wood that is on an action space, you receive 2 additional wood.

I80.name: Spinney
I80.text: Whenever another player uses the "3 Wood" action space, they must give you one of the wood.

I81.name: Wooden Hut Extension
I81.text: When you play this card, immediately extend your wooden hut by 1 room. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

I82.name: Wooden Crane
I82.reason: the round restriction (5-7, 10-11) identifies a specific 'Take 1 Stone' card by the fixed round it's revealed on the original board; this engine shuffles stage-card order within each stage, so there is no stable mapping from those literal round numbers to western_quarry/eastern_quarry that would let the condition be tested faithfully.
I82.text: Whenever you use one of the "1 Stone" action spaces that are placed in rounds 5-7 and 10-11, you receive 1 additional stone. If you pay 1 food, you can take 2 additional stone instead of 1.

I83.name: Wooden Path
I83.text: The player with the most valuable street receives 2 bonus points when scoring.

I84.name: Chicken Coop
I84.text: Place 1 food each on the next 8 remaining round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

I85.name: Cooking Corner
I85.text: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: vegetables → 4 food; sheep → 2 food; wild boar → 3 food; cattle → 4 food. Whenever you use a bread baking action, you may convert: grain → 3 food.

I86.name: Corn Storehouse
I86.text: Whenever you have empty fields after the field phase of the harvest, you can sow grain in them immediately. Place 1 fewer grain from the supply on these fields.

I87.name: Flagon
I87.text: Whenever the Well A10 is built or upgraded to a Village Well I66 , you receive 4 food, and the other players receive 1 food each. If the Well has already been built, everyone receives the food when the Flagon is played.

I88.name: Lasso
I88.text: You can place exactly two people immediately after one another, if at least one of them uses the "1 Wild Boar", "1 Cattle" or "1 Sheep" action space.

I89.name: Clay Path
I89.text: The player with the most valuable street receives 2 bonus points when scoring.

I90.name: Planter Box
I90.text: Whenever you sow, each of the fields you sow that is orthogonally adjacent to a room in your home gets additional goods from the general supply: 2 extra grain on grain fields, and 1 extra vegetable on vegetable fields .

I91.name: Ladder
I91.text: You need 1 less reed to extend or renovate your home or to build the Water Mill I103 , Half-timbered House E21 , Chicken Coop I84 , Holiday House I71 , Mansion K144 , or Corn Storehouse I86 .

I92.name: Manure
I92.text: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can take 1 grain or vegetable from each of your fields and place it in your personal supply.

I93.name: Milking Shed
I93.text: In each harvest, at the beginning of the field phase, count the total number of sheep and cattle in all the players' farms. You receive 1 food for each fifth sheep and for each third cattle.

I94.name: Paved Road
I94.text: The player with the most valuable street receives 2 Bonus points when scoring.

I95.name: Fish Trap
I95.text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" action space or receive reed on an action space, you receive 1 additional food.

I96.name: Reed Exchange
I96.text: When you play this card, you receive 2 reeds. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

I97.name: Slaughterhouse
I97.reason: reacts to another player converting an animal to food during feeding, and moves the owner to the end of the feeding turn order; _apply_feed applies conversions directly with no fired event for any card to observe, and there is no mechanism to reorder the feeding sequence.
I97.text: Whenever another player converts 1 or more animals to food, you receive 1 food from the supply. During the feeding phase of the harvest, you are the last player to take a turn.

I98.name: Schnaps Distillery
I98.text: During the feeding phase of the harvest, you can use the Schnaps Distillery to convert at most 1 vegetable into 4 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point each for your 5th and 6th vegetables.

I99.name: Straw-thatched Roof
I99.text: You no longer need reed when you extend or renovate your home.

K105.name: Acreage
K105.text: When you sow, you can plant grain on this card. There can be up to 2 stacks of grain on this card, as shown.

K106.name: Bakehouse
K106.text: Whenever you use a bread baking action, you can use the Bakehouse to convert up to 2 grain into 5 food each. When you play this card, you can also bake bread immediately.

K107.name: Lumber
K107.text: When you play this card, you receive 3 wood. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

K108.name: Beehive
K108.text: Place 2 food on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

K109.name: Spit Roast
K109.reason: requires detecting an animal->food conversion during the feeding phase specifically. Neither _apply_feed's conversion loop nor _apply_accommodate's cook loop fires any card hook (same gap noted for C053 in deck_c_minors.py) -- there is no event to react to.
K109.text: Whenever you convert at least 1 animal to food during the feeding phase of the harvest, you receive 1 additional food.

K110.name: Brewery
K110.text: During the feeding phase of the harvest, you can use the Brewery to convert at most 1 grain to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for your ninth grain.

K111.name: Bread Paddle
K111.text: Whenever you play an occupation, you may also bake bread.

K112.name: Bookshelf
K112.reason: requires granting food to the player BEFORE the occupation's food cost is deducted, so it can enable playing an occupation otherwise unaffordable. _play_occupation calls self._pay(...) before any hook fires (play/occupation_played both fire after payment), so there is no hook point earlier than the deduction.
K112.text: Whenever you play 1 occupation, you receive 3 food before you pay the costs of the occupation.

K113.name: Flail
K113.text: Whenever you use the "Plough 1 Field" or "Plough Field and Sow" action spaces, you can additionally bake bread.

K114.name: Duck Pond
K114.text: Place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

K115.name: Swing Plough
K115.text: Twice during the game, when you use the "Plough 1 Field" action space, you can plough 3 fields instead of 1.

K116.name: Granary
K116.text: Place 1 grain on each remaining space for rounds 8, 10 and 12. At the start of these rounds, you receive the grain.

K117.name: Greenhouse
K117.text: Add 4 and 7 tothe number of the current round and place 1 vegetable on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 1 food to take the vegetable.

K118.name: Liquid Manure
K118.text: Whenever you sow, place 1 additional grain or vegetable from the general supply on each new stack you sow.

K119.name: Crooked Plough
K119.text: Once during the game, when you use the "Plough 1 Field" action space, you can plough 3 fields instead of 1.

K120.name: House Goat
K120.text: In each feeding phase, you receive 1 food. Apart from the goat, you cannot hold any other animal in your home.

K121.name: Sawhorse
K121.text: The next stable you place in your farmyard, as well as your 3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th and 15th fence, costs you nothing.

K122.name: Sawmill
K122.text: Each harvest, you may convert up to 1 wood to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/5 wood.

K123.name: Wooden Strongbox
K123.text: At the end of the game, you get 2 bonus points if your home contains 5 rooms, or 4 bonus points if you have 6 or more rooms.

K124.name: Yoke
K124.reason: the bonus scales with how many 'plough' cards (of any type, played by ANY player, including cards from other compendium decks such as Harrow I68) are in play. No cross-deck registry of which minor/occupation instances count as a 'plough' exists or is exposed to a deck module -- hardcoding only this module's own plough cards (Swing Plough/Crooked Plough) would silently undercount.
K124.text: When you play this card, you can immediately plough 1 field for each plough or Harrow I68 that has been played by any player.

K125.name: Broom
K125.text: Discard all the remaining minor improvements in your hand, and draw 7 new minor improvements. You can play 1 more minor improvement immediately.

K126.name: Landing Net
K126.text: Whenever you receive reed on an action space, you receive an additional 2 food. This is reduced to 1 food if you receive other building resources as well as reed.

K127.name: Clapper
K127.text: Whenever you use one of the family growth action spaces (or if you already have used one of these spaces in this round when you play the Clapper), place 1 additional grain on all of your fields that already contains at least 1 grain.

K128.name: Cooking Hearth
K128.text: At any time, you can convert goods to food as follows: vegetables → 3 food; sheep → 2 food; wild boar → 3 food; cattle → 4 food. Whenever you use a bread baking action, you may convert: grain → 3 food.

K129.name: Corn Sheaf
K129.text: When you play this card, you receive 1 grain. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

K130.name: Herb Garden
K130.text: Place 1 food on each of the next 5 round spaces. At the start of each round, you receive the food.

K131.name: Clay Pit
K131.text: Whenever you use the "Day Labourer" action space, you receive 3 additional clay.

K132.name: Clay Hut Extension
K132.text: When you play this card, immediately extend your clay hut by 1 room. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

K133.name: Milking Stool
K133.text: Whenever you have cattle during the field phase of a harvest, you receive food: 1 food if you have 1 or 2 cattle; 2 food if you have 3 or 4 cattle; 3 food if you have 5 or more cattle. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for every 2 cattle.

K134.name: Ox Team
K134.text: When you play this card, count how many complete rounds are left to be played. You can plough this many fields, up to a maximum of 3.

K135.name: Horse
K135.text: You receive 2 bonus points for any one type of animal missing from your farm at the end of the game.

K136.name: Brushwood Roof
K136.text: Whenever you extend or renovate your home, you can replace 1 or 2 reed with the same amount of wood.

K137.name: Turnip Field
K137.text: When you sow, you can plant vegetables on this card as you would on a field. When you play this card, you can also carry out a sowing action.

K138.name: Reed Hut
K138.reason: places an extra, non-scoring family member token that can act and must be fed but isn't part of the family -- the guest-token/extra-people mechanic the guide marks unsupported.
K138.text: Place one family member token that you have not yet brought into the game onto this card, where it will live for the rest of the game. It can take actions and must be fed, but it is not worth any points while living in the Reed Hut.

K139.name: Sleeping Corner
K139.reason: lets the player use a family-growth action space another player has already placed on this round -- placing on an occupied space is the guide's unsupported mechanic.
K139.text: You can use any "Family Growth" action space, even if another player has already placed a person there.

K140.name: Swan Lake
K140.text: Place 1 food each on the next 5 remaining round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

K141.name: Boar Breeding
K141.text: When you play this card,you receive 1 wild boar. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

K142.name: Stone Cart
K142.text: Place 1 stone on each remaining even-numbered round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the stone.

K143.name: Stone Exchange
K143.text: When you play this card, you receive 2 stone. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

K144.name: Mansion
K144.text: At the end of the game, you receive 2 bonus points for each room in your stone house. In total, you receive 4 points per room instead of the usual 2 points.

K145.name: Forest Pasture
K145.text: This card can hold an unlimited number of wild boar.

K146.name: Loom
K146.text: Whenever you have sheep during the field phase of a harvest, you receive food: 1 food if you have 1 to 3 sheep; 2 food for 4 to 6 sheep; or 3 food for 7 or more sheep. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for every 3 sheep.

K266.name: Serf
K266.text: Whenever you use the "Sow and Bake Bread" action space, you receive 1 grain before taking the action(s). Alternatively, you can exchange 1 grain for 1 vegetable.

K267.name: Adoptive Parents
K267.text: When you take a family growth action, you can pay 1 food to immediately place the offspring in your home. This allows you to take an action with it this round. If you do this, the offspring does not count as "newborn".

K268.name: Pieceworker
K268.text: Whenever you receive wood, clay, reed, stone or grain on an action space, you can buy one more of the same good for 1 food. Whenever you receive vegetable(s) on an action space, you can buy one more for 2 food.

K269.name: Acrobat
K269.text: Whenever you use the "Traveling players" action on an action space, after all of the players have finished their turns you may move that person to one of the "Take 1 Grain", "Plough 1 Field" and "Plough Field and Sow" action spaces, if it's free, and take the action.

K270.name: Wet Nurse
K270.text: Whenever you build room(s), you may grow your family by up to the number of rooms that you build, if you have the room to house the new family members. This costs 1 food per person.

K271.name: Educator
K271.text: Whenever another player plays an occupation card, you can pay 3 food to play one yourself. From your 4th occupation, this only costs 2 food.

K272.name: Frame Builder
K272.text: In each renovation, you may replace exactly 1 clay or 1 stone with 1 wood. In each extension, you may replace exactly 2 clay or 2 stone with 1 wood.

K273.name: Basin Maker
K273.reason: requires reacting to converting wild boar into food; no event fires when animals are cooked (neither the accommodate-prompt "cook" branch nor the feeding "via: cook" conversion calls cards.fire/fire_player).
K273.text: For each wild boar that you convert into food, you can place up to 2 wood from your personal supply on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each wood on this card except the 1st, 4th, 7th and 10th.

K274.name: Organic Farmer
K274.text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each of your pastures that contains at least 1 animal, but could contain at least 3 more animals than it does.

K275.name: Perpetual Student
K275.reason: requires the normal Lessons-space play-occupation action to accept "play a random card from my hand" instead of a specific card id; the only way to add that is editing _play_occupation's parameter handling in engine.py, which per GUIDE.md we don't do per-card.
K275.text: Whenever you can play an occupation card, you can ask another player to randomly draw one of your occupation cards instead of choosing one yourself. If you do this, you receive 3 food before you pay the costs of the card, but you must play the card that the other player draws.

K276.name: Constable
K276.text: You receive wood immediately when you play this card: if played in round 12 or 13, take 1 wood; if played in round 9, 10, or 11, take 2 wood; if played in round 6, 7, or 8, take 3 wood; if played before round 6, take 4 wood. At the end of the game, any player who has no negative points receives 5 bonus points.

K277.name: Manufacturer
K277.reason: requires redirecting specific major improvements (Joinery/Pottery/Basketmaker's Workshop) through the minor-improvement action-space flow, plus a player-chosen 2-resource discount; no hook lets a card change which action-space channel builds an improvement.
K277.text: Once you have a clay hut or a stone house, the Joinery A7 , Pottery A8 and Basketmaker's Workshop A9 are minor improvements for you and their cost is reduced by 2 building resources of your choice.

K278.name: Forester
K278.text: Whenever you use a sowing action, you can plant wood on this card. There can be up to 3 stacks of wood on this card, as shown. The wood is treated the same as sown grain and is harvested during the Field phase.

K279.name: Scholar
K279.text: Once you have a stone house, at the start of a round you can always either pay 1 food to play an occupation card or play an improvement card by paying its costs.

K280.name: Tanner
K280.reason: requires reacting to converting wild boar or cattle into food (same gap as K273: no cook/convert event exists).
K280.text: When you convert wild boar or cattle to food, place them on this card. At the end of the game, you receive bonus points if you have animal markers on this card. For the wild boar on this card, you receive: 6 or more wild boar, 3 points; 4 or 5 wild boar, 2 points; 2 or 3 wild boar, 1 point. For the cattle on this card, you receive: 4 or more cattle, 3 points; 3 cattle, 2 points; 2 cattle, 1 point.

K281.name: Traveling Salesman
K281.reason: requires letting a "minor improvement" action space build a major instead, or a "major or minor" space play 2 minors; this needs re-implementing the build/play parameter shapes of _resolve_space outside the normal placement flow (same category of gap as B150 in deck B).
K281.text: Whenever you select a "minor improvement" action on an action space, you can play a major instead of a minor improvement. If you select a "major or minor improvement" action on an action space, you can play 2 minor improvements.

K282.name: House Steward
K282.text: You receive wood immediately when you play this card: if played in round 12 or 13, take 1 wood; if played in round 9, 10, or 11, take 2 wood; if played in round 6, 7, or 8, take 3 wood; if played before round 6, take 4 wood. At the end of the game, the player(s) with the most rooms in their home receive 3 bonus points each.

K283.name: Wood Deliveryman
K283.text: Place 1 wood on each remaining space for rounds 8 to 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the wood.

K284.name: Wood Distributor
K284.text: At the start of the work phase of each round, you can distribute the wood from the "3 Wood" action space as evenly as possible onto the neighbouring "1 Clay", "1 Reed" and "Fishing" spaces. When you play this card, you receive 2 wood.

K285.name: Tinsmith
K285.text: You can convert clay into food at any time. You receive 1 food per clay. If any player has built a Well A10 , you receive 3 food for each 2 clay instead.

K286.name: Smallholder
K286.text: Your pastures that can hold 2 animals can hold 3 animals. While you have at most 2 fields, add 1 extra grain or vegetable from the general supply to each field you sow.

K287.name: Storehouse Clerk
K287.text: Whenever you have at least 5 stone at the start of a round, you receive 1 extra stone. If you have at least 6 reed, you receive 1 reed. If you have at least 7 clay, you receive 1 clay. If you have at least 8 wood, you receive 1 wood.

K288.name: Storehouse Keeper
K288.text: Whenever you use a person's action to take reed and stone at the same time, you also receive your choice of 1 clay or 1 grain.

K289.name: Countryman
K289.text: After all players have placed their people, you may move one of your people from a "Take 1 Grain" or "Take 1 Vegetable" action space to a free action space with a "sow" action.

K290.name: Clay Worker
K290.text: Whenever you use an action of a person to take wood or clay, you also receive 1 additional clay.

K291.name: Lover
K291.text: When you play this card, immediately carry out a "Family growth even without room" action (similar to the round card from stage 5). Playing this card costs you an additional 4 food.

K292.name: Market Woman
K292.text: Whenever you receive vegetables through a person's action or through a minor improvement, you receive an additional 2 grain.

K293.name: Ploughman
K293.text: Add 4, 7 and 10 to the number of the current round and place 1 field on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can plough that field by paying 1 food.

K294.name: Brushwood Collector
K294.text: You may replace the required reed with a total of 1 wood for any renovation or for each new room. (You use brushwood to make the roof.)

K295.name: Cattle Breeder
K295.text: Your cattle breed at the end of round 12, if there is space for the calf. When you play this card, you receive 1 cattle.

K296.name: Seed Seller
K296.text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" action space, you receive 1 additional grain. When you play this card, you receive 1 grain.

K297.name: Sheep Farmer
K297.text: Whenever you take sheep with one of your people, you receive an additional sheep from the general supply. You can exchange 3 sheep for 1 cattle and 1 wild boar at any time (except during the breeding phase).

K298.name: Shepherd Boy
K298.text: Once you live in a stone house, place 1 sheep on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the sheep.

K299.name: Slaughterman
K299.reason: requires reacting to any player converting animals to food (no cook/convert event, as K273/K280) and reordering the feeding turn sequence; this engine's feeding phase has no turn order at all (get_waiting_for returns every not-yet-fed player at once), so "feed last" has no mechanical meaning here.
K299.text: Whenever another player converts 1 or more animals into food, you receive 1 food from the general supply. In the feeding phase of the harvest, you are the last player to feed your family (so you can benefit if other players slaughter).

K300.name: Schnaps Distiller
K300.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can convert up to 1 vegetable to 5 food.

K301.name: Wood Carver
K301.reason: the wood discount applies once per ROUND across any qualifying purchase (not once per item, unlike Stonecutter's unconditional per-item discount); cost_mod is a pure function queried speculatively for validity checks as well as at actual payment, so a stateful "already used this round" flag can't be consumed safely there without also corrupting cost previews.
K301.text: In each round, you pay 1 wood less for one of the following: an improvement, a room of a wooden hut, a stable or a fence.

K302.name: Pig Whisperer
K302.text: Add 4, 7 and 10 to the number of the current round and place 1 wild boar on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the wild boar.

K303.name: Stone Breaker
K303.text: At any time, you can renovate your clay hut to a stone house without using a "Renovation" action space.

K304.name: Veterinarian
K304.text: When you play this card, place 4 sheep cubes, 3 wild boar cubes and 2 cattle cubes in a container. At the start of each round, draw two animals. If they are the same, keep one. Return the 1 or 2 animals to the container.

K305.name: Animal Handler
K305.text: Place 1 sheep on the space for round 7, 1 wild boar on round 10 and 1 cattle on round 14. At the start of these rounds, you can buy the animal for 1 food.

K306.name: Animal Tamer
K306.text: You can keep 1 animal in each room of your home. You may keep more than 1 type of animal in your home.

K307.name: Animal Breeder
K307.text: Whenever you fence unused spaces to create at least one new pasture, you can buy a pair of animals: 2 sheep for 1 food, 2 wild boar for 2 food, or 2 cattle for 3 food.

K308.name: Foreman
K308.reason: grants "place 1 food on an action space of your choice" at the start of each round; round_start hooks must not prompt (a prompt pending at round start blocks every player's placements per GUIDE.md), and there is no sane default space to auto-select -- the whole value of the card is the open-ended choice.
K308.text: At the start of the work phase of each round, you can place 1 food from the general supply on an action space of your choice.

K309.name: Weaver
K309.text: Whenever you have at least 2 sheep at the start of the work phase of a round, you receive 1 food.

K310.name: Resource Seller
K310.text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 stone, clay, stone, clay, reed, clay, wood on this card. You receive the top marker when you receive that type of building resource.

K311.name: Magician
K311.text: Whenever you use your last person to choose the "Traveling Players" action on an action space, you receive an additional 1 grain and 1 food.

K312.name: Fence Overseer
K312.text: Once each round, for a cost of 1 food, you can immediately fence a pasture of 1 farmyard space around a stable that you have just built. You do not need to pay wood for the fences.

K339.name: Pelts
K339.reason: requires detecting each individual animal->food conversion during feeding (same unfired-hook gap as K109/C053) AND a novel per-room food-storage mechanic (max 1 food token per room, scored as bonus points) that nothing in the engine tracks.
K339.text: For each animal that you convert to food and return to the general supply, you may place 1 food from your personal supply in 1 of your rooms. You may have a maximum of 1 food in each room. You cannot use these food any more, but each is worth 1 bonus point at the end of the game.

K342.name: Animal Trainer
K342.text: Whenever you take food from a "Traveling players" space, you may immediately use it to buy animals: Pay 2 food for each sheep or wild boar and 3 food for each cattle.

M001.name: Peat-charcoal Kiln
M001.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M001.text: Whenever you use the "Cut Peat" special action, you receive 1 additional fuel. If you have at least one horse at the time, you receive another 1 fuel. In the final harvest, you can exchange 3 fuel for 1 bonus point, or 5 fuel for 2 bonus points.

M002.name: Forester's Lodge
M002.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M002.text: Whenever you use the "Fell Trees" special action, you receive 1 additional wood. If you have at least one horse at the time, you receive another 1 wood. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each forest in your farmyard.

M003.name: Riding Stables
M003.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M003.text: Place 1 food on the round space for each remaining round. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food, as long as you have at least 2 horses.

M006.name: Museum of the Moors
M006.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M006.text: The following major improvements are cheaper for you: Well A10 , by 1 stone; Joinery A7 , by 1 wood; Pottery A8 , by 1 clay; Basketmaker's Workshop A9 , by 1 reed; Clay Oven A5 , by 1 clay; Stone Oven A6 , by 1 stone; Forester's Lodge M002 , by 1 clay.

M007.name: Furnace
M007.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M007.text: You immediately receive 2 fuel. When heating, you heat one room fewer than you have.

M008.name: Heating Stove
M008.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M008.text: Regardless of its size, you need at most 1 fuel to heat your entire home.

M009.name: Village Church
M009.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M009.text: You immediately receive 2 food. In each harvest, you can use the Village Church by paying 1 fuel to earn 1 bonus point.

M010.name: Furniture Stall
M010.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M010.text: At any time, you can exchange wood for clay. Return the wood to the supply and take the same number of clay.

M011.name: Ceramics Stall
M011.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M011.text: At any time, you can exchange clay for wood. Return the clay to the supply and take the same number of wood.

M012.name: Basket Stall
M012.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M012.text: At any time, you can exchange reed for other building resources. Return the reed to the supply and take the same number of other building resources of your choice.

M015.name: Fallow Fields
M015.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M015.text: Place 2 food from the general supply on each of up to 3 empty fields. You cannot harvest the food. You receive it when you sow in the fields.

M016.name: Peat Burn-off
M016.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M016.text: You immediately receive 1 fuel. In addition you can (optionally) immediately exchange 1 moor tile for a field tile. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M017.name: Cattle Collar
M017.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M017.text: Whenever you use the "Plough Field" action or the "Slash and Burn" special action, you can immediately place 1 additional field tile on an empty farmyard space. To do this, you must have at least 1 cattle in your farm.

M018.name: Surveyor's Map
M018.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M018.text: Place a field tile on the space for round 11, a moor tile on round 12, and a forest tile on round 13. At the start of the rounds, you must either place the tile on an empty space in your farm or return it to the supply.

M019.name: Sowing Machine
M019.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M019.text: Whenever you use a special action, you may immediately take a "Sow" action as long as you have at least 2 horses.

M020.name: Peat Fertilizer
M020.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M020.text: Whenever you use the "Cut Peat" special action, you can immediately take a "Sow" action.

M021.name: Peat Ash Fertilizer
M021.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M021.text: Whenever you use the "Cut Peat" special action, you can place 1 additional grain on all fields and farmyard spaces where grain is growing. In addition, you can place 1 additional vegetable on all fields and farmyard spaces where vegetables are planted.

M022.name: Nosebag
M022.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M022.text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" action, you also receive 1 horse.

M023.name: Ploughhorse Market
M023.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M023.text: Whenever you use a person to take a "Plough fields" action, you can buy 1 horse for 1 food immediately afterwards.

M024.name: Large Livestock Market
M024.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M024.text: You can immediately exchange 2 sheep for 1 cattle and 1 horse. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M025.name: Special Pasture
M025.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M025.text: Immediately fence 1 empty farmyard space (or a farmyard space with a stable) with 4 fences. You need not pay wood for these. The fenced space may not border your existing pastures.

M026.name: Home Wood
M026.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M026.text: With this improvement, you can keep 1 animal (except a sheep) on each of your forest tiles.

M027.name: Horse Trough
M027.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M027.text: You can keep up to 2 horses in an unused farmyard space adjacent to your home.

M028.name: Open Grill
M028.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M028.text: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: vegetable → 2 food; sheep → 2 food; wild boar → 3 food; cattle → 3 food; horse → 2 food. Whenever you use a bread baking action, you may convert: grain → 2 food.

M029.name: Pot Roast Recipe
M029.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M029.text: You can use the Fireplace A1/A2 or Cooking Hearth A3/A4 major improvements to convert horses to food. You receive 2 food for each horse.

M030.name: Grain Distillery
M030.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M030.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can convert exactly 1 fuel and 1 grain to 5 food. In the final harvest, you can convert any number of pairs of 1 fuel and 1 grain from your supply to 1 bonus point per pair.

M031.name: Forest Kindergarten
M031.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M031.text: Whenever you take any "family growth" action, you receive 1 food for each of your farmyard spaces that contains a forest tile.

M032.name: Pheromones
M032.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M032.text: You immediately receive 1 food. In addition, each player who has at least one stable or pasture receives 2 food.

M033.name: Malthouse
M033.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M033.text: Whenever you use the "Cut Peat" special action, you can also convert 1 grain to 4 food.

M034.name: Chimney Hood
M034.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M034.text: You immediately receive as much food as you would receive from one of your baking improvements if you baked 1 grain into bread. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M035.name: Village Hall
M035.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M035.text: In each round where you finish the work phase without a special action card in front of you, you receive 1 food during the returning home phase,

M036.name: Healing Clay
M036.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M036.text: You immediately receive 1 food. Whenever you take the "Infirmary" action with a person who is marked with a bed token, you receive 1 additional food.

M037.name: Arid Field
M037.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M037.text: Whenever you use the "Cut Peat" special action, place 1 fuel and 1 food from the general supply on the emptied farmyard space. You receive the token once the space is no longer empty.

M038.name: Birthing House
M038.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M038.text: Whenever you use any "family growth" action, you receive 1 bonus point as well as 1 fuel and 1 food.

M039.name: Barge
M039.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M039.text: Place, alternately, 1 fuel or 1 food on each remaining round space, beginning with 1 fuel. At the start of each round, you receive the token.

M040.name: Peat Barge
M040.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M040.text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" action, you also receive 2 fuel.

M041.name: Peat Sled
M041.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M041.text: Add your choice of 2, 4, 7, or 10 to the current round number. On the corresponding round space, place 3, 4, 5, or 6 fuel (depending on which number you added). At the start of that round, you receive the fuel.

M042.name: Peat Iron
M042.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M042.text: Whenever you have at least 2 moor tiles in your farmyard at the start of a harvest, you receive 1 fuel.

M043.name: Spinning Mill
M043.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M043.text: For every 2 sheep that you have in the field phase of each harvest, you pay 1 fuel less to heat your home in the feeding phase of that round.

M044.name: Built-in Oven
M044.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M044.text: You no longer need to pay any heating costs.

M045.name: Coal Seam
M045.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M045.text: You immediately receive 1 fuel. Whenever you use the "Hiring Fair" special action or the "Day Labourer" action space, you also receive 1 fuel.

M046.name: Oven Damper
M046.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M046.text: You immediately receive 3 fuel. The Clay Oven A5 , Stone Oven A6 , Furnace M007 and Heating Stove M008 major improvements and the Built-in Oven M044 minor improvement are each worth 1 additional bonus point to you.

M047.name: Draughthorses
M047.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M047.text: Whenever you take exactly 3 wood from an action space, you can pay 1 food to receive 1 additional wood. Whenever you take 4 or more wood from an action space, you can pay 1 food to receive 2 additional wood. To do either of these, you must have at least 1 horse.

M048.name: Forest Path
M048.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M048.text: You immediately receive 3 wood. Once this card has been passed, the player who has it at the end of the game receives -1 point. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M049.name: Moor Birch Tree
M049.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M049.text: Whenever you use the "Cut Peat" special action, you also receive 2 wood.

M050.name: River Clay
M050.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M050.text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" action space, you also receive 2 clay.

M051.name: Willow Bank
M051.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M051.text: Whenever you use the "Fell Trees" special action, you also receive 1 reed.

M052.name: Stone Quarry
M052.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M052.text: Place 5 stone on this card. Whenever you use the "Hiring Fair" special action, you also receive 1 stone from this card.

M053.name: Hardware Store
M053.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M053.text: Place 3 usage counters on this card. At any time, you can return one usage counter to the supply and take 1 of each building resource that you have none of.

M054.name: Basic Supplies
M054.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M054.text: Immediately take goods and tokens from the general supply until you have at least 1 fuel, 1 food, 1 wood, 1 clay, 1 reed, 1 stone, and 1 grain in your personal supply. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M055.name: Advance Payment
M055.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M055.text: You immediately receive 1 fuel, 1 food, 1 wood, 1 clay, 1 reed, 1 stone, 1 sheep, and 1 grain.

M056.name: Peat Moss
M056.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M056.text: You require 2 less wood to build wooden hut rooms.

M057.name: Farmhands' Quarters
M057.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M057.text: Whenever you acquire a major improvement, you can save 1 building resource of your choice by paying 1 fuel instead. Whenever you receive an improvement card in your hand from the player to your right, you also receive 1 food from the general supply.

M058.name: Living History Museum
M058.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M058.text: The following major improvements are cheaper for you: Village Church M009 , by 1 stone; Furniture Stall M010 , by 1 wood; Ceramics Stall M011 , by 1 clay; Basket Stall M012 , by 1 reed; Furnace M007 , by 1 clay; Heating Stove M008 , by 1 stone; Riding Stables M003 , by 1 wood.

M059.name: Register of Craftsmen
M059.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M059.text: Immediately acquire the Joinery A7 , Pottery A8 , or Basketmaker's Workshop A9 , without placing a person. You pay 1 stone less for it. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M060.name: Tinker
M060.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M060.text: If you have at least one of the Joinery A7 , Pottery A8 , or Basketmaker's Workshop A9 major improvements, you immediately receive 1 wood, 1 clay, 1 reed, and 1 stone. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M061.name: Workbench
M061.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M061.text: You receive 3 wood, 2 clay, and 1 reed in the field phase of each of the last 2 harvests.

M062.name: Taps
M062.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M062.text: You can take an available special action card even after you have already placed all your person markers, when it would be your turn again. Then the work phase is over for you.

M063.name: Tool Shed
M063.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M063.text: Whenever you take the special action card with "Slash and Burn" and "Cut Peat", and take one of these special actions, you can take both of these special actions (in whichever order you choose).

M064.name: Farm Extension
M064.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M064.text: This card gives you 2 additional spaces which you can use as normal farmyard spaces. Place it beside your farmyard. Each of the new spaces must border an existing farmyard space. This expands your famyard by 2 spaces.

M065.name: Thicket
M065.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M065.text: Choose up to 2 forest tiles, and place 1 additional forest tile on top of each of them. You can remove 1 tile with the "Fell Trees" special action-you cannot use the "Slash and Burn" action on spaces with 2 forest tiles.

M066.name: Swamp
M066.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M066.text: Place 1 moor tile on the space for round 12. At the start of this round, you can take the tile and place it on an empty space in your farmyard.

M067.name: Clear Felling
M067.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M067.text: You immediately receive 2 wood. You can immediately turn over up to 2 forest tiles to show the moor side, as long as there is nothing except the forest tile on those spaces. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M068.name: Reforestation
M068.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M068.text: Immediately place 1 forest tile on an empty space in your farmyard. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M069.name: Bog Body
M069.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M069.text: The owner of the Museum of the Moors M006 and the owner of the Living History Museum M058 each receive 1 bonus point at the end of the game.

M070.name: Administration
M070.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M070.text: You immediately receive 2 food. In the last harvest, for each major improvement that you have, you can exchange 1 food for 1 bonus point.

M071.name: Leather Saddle
M071.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M071.text: Whenever you have at least 3 horses, you receive 1 bonus point for each cattle that you convert to food.

M072.name: Wedding Coach
M072.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M072.text: You can immediately carry out a "family growth even without space in your home" action, but you do not place a person to take this action. Place the offspring on this card until the returning home phase.

M073.name: Forest Hut
M073.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M073.text: Immediately place 1 guest marker on one of your forest tiles. You will receive the guest marker when you use a special action to clear that forest tile. Then, in that same round, you can place the guest marker as you would a person. At the end of that round, return the guest marker to the supply.

M074.name: Farm Cart
M074.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M074.text: Whenever you take at least 5 wood, 4 clay, 3 reeds, or 2 stone from an action space, you also receive 1 grain.

M075.name: Moor Fire
M075.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M075.text: Once you only have 1 remaining moor tile, you can exchange it for a field tile at any time.

M076.name: Deep Plough
M076.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M076.text: Immediately place 1 moor tile on an empty space in your farmyard. Whenever you use a person to take the "Plough fields" action, you can also exchange one moor tile for 1 field tile.

M077.name: Wild Fields
M077.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M077.text: You can immediately place up to 2 field tiles on empty spaces in your farmyard-on the condition that neither tile borders any other field at the time when it is placed.

M078.name: No-Till Farming
M078.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M078.text: You can plant grain or vegetables on up to 2 empty farmyard spaces. These spaces do not count as fields when scoring, but as empty spaces.

M079.name: Nature's Fertilizer
M079.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M079.text: Whenever you use the "Slash and Burn" special action, you immediately receive a "Sow" action for that field only. This also applies if you turn 1 moor tile into a field using the Moor Fire M075 or Peat Burn-off M016 minor improvement.

M080.name: Peat Hut
M080.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M080.text: This card serves as a room for one of your people. It must be heated with 1 fuel during the feeding phase of each harvest. You can use renovate action to discard this card and add 1 room to your wooden hut.

M081.name: Flatboat
M081.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M081.text: Place, alternately, 1 fuel or 1 horse on each of the next 7 remaining round spaces, beginning with 1 fuel. At the start of each round, you receive the fuel or the horse.

M082.name: Livestock Market
M082.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M082.text: You can immediately exchange up to 3 animals for the next most valuable animal. You must swap them al at the same time, and you can only take the new animals if you have room for all of them on your farm. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M083.name: Cattle Stall
M083.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M083.text: Add 2, 4, 6, 8 to the current round. Place 1 animal on each corresponding round space. You choose which animal goes on which space, but all the animals must be different. At the start of these rounds, you can buy the animal for 1 food.

M084.name: Night Pasture
M084.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M084.text: You can keep 3 animals of your choice on this card, and other players can keep 1. All the animals on this card count as yours when animals breed. You are always the last to play during the breeding phase.

M085.name: Nature Reserve
M085.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M085.text: Immediately fence 1 farmyard space which contains a forest or moor tile. You do not pay any wood for these fences. Once there are no tiles left on the space, it becomes a pasture.

M086.name: Building Plan
M086.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M086.text: Whenever you build 2 rooms at once with the "Build rooms" action, you can immediately build 2 stables from your personal supply.

M087.name: Horse Butcher
M087.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M087.text: At any time, you may convert animals to food as follows: sheep → 1 food; wild boar → 2 food; cattle → 3 food; horse → 2 food; 2 horses → 5 food.

M088.name: Butcher's Block
M088.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M088.text: This card cannot be played in a round that is followed by a harvest. All other players must and you may immediately convert one animal of their (your) choice to food. Sheep provide 1 food, wild boar 2 food, cattle 3 food.

M089.name: Fire Brigade
M089.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M089.text: You immediately receive 2 food. If you still have forest tiles on at least 2 farm yard spaces, you get bonus points: 1 fewer point than you have forests, with a maximum of 4 points.

M090.name: Wild Harvest
M090.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M090.text: You immediately receive 1 food. At the start of each harvest, shuffle all the start cards and draw 1. The start cards are numbered from 1 to 9. If the number on the card is equal to or less than the number of forest tiles in your farmyard, you receive 1 food.

M091.name: Fish Smokehouse
M091.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M091.text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" action space, you can pay 1 fuel to receive an additional 3 food.

M092.name: Loan
M092.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M092.text: At the start of each harvest, shuffle all the start cards and draw 1. The start cards are numbered from 1 to 9. If the number on the card is equal to or less than the amount of clay you have in your supply, the 2 food that you paid are returned to you with interest: you receive 6 food but must pass this improvement to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M093.name: Fallow Land
M093.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M093.text: Whenever you use the "Fell Trees" or "Cut Peat" special actions to clear a space in your farmyard, place 1 Food on the space. You receive the food when the space is no longer empty.

M094.name: Hay Wagon
M094.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M094.text: Immediately after you take 3 wood, 3 clay, 2 reed, or 2 stone from an action space, you can take a "Build room(s)" or "Renovate" action without placing a person.

M095.name: Peat Bath
M095.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M095.text: Whenever you use the "Infirmary" action space with a person, count how many moor tiles are in your farmyard. Place 1 food on the next remaining round space(s) up to that number. At the beginning of those rounds, you receive the food.

M096.name: Edge of the Forest
M096.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M096.text: For each border between a forest tile and a field in your farmyard, you receive 1 food. For each border between a forest tile and a moor tile in your farmyard, you receive 1 fuel. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M097.name: Ecological Niche
M097.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M097.text: If there is at least one type of animal that only you own, you receive 2 food. If you are the only player who has planted grain, you receive 1 food. If you are the only player who has planted vegetables, you receive 1 food. You receive 1 fuel is you have more forest tiles than any other player and 1 fuel if you have more moor tiles than any other player. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M098.name: Winter Storehouse
M098.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M098.text: Place 3 usage counters on this card. At any time (even while you are taking an action) you can return 1 usage counter and take fuel and food from the supply until you have at least 2 fuel and 2 food.

M099.name: Lawn Turf
M099.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M099.text: If you have at least 3 unused spaces on your farmyard, you immediately receive 1 fuel for each unused space except the first 2, up to a maximum of 5 fuel. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M100.name: Firewood
M100.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M100.text: You immediately receive 1 fuel. Whenever you convert at least 1 wood to fuel during a harvest to heat your home, you need a total of 1 fuel fewer.

M101.name: Peat Pellets
M101.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M101.text: You immediately receive 1 fuel for each remaining moor tile in your farmyard. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M102.name: Fuel Storage
M102.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M102.text: Add 1, 5, and 9 to the current round. Place 1 wood on each corresponding round space. Add 3, 7, and 11 to the current round. Place 1 fuel on each corresponding round space. At the start of each round, you receive the wood or fuel.

M103.name: Bog Pony
M103.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M103.text: At any time, you can lie one of your horses that is standing up on its side and take 2 fuel. This horse does not count during the breeding phase of the harvest. At the end of the game, it is worth only half a point, but it can (like other horses) be converted into food with an appropriate improvement.

M104.name: Drying Field
M104.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M104.text: Whenever you use the "Cut Peat" special action, add 3 to the current round. Place 2 fuel on the corresponding round space. At the start of the round, you receive the fuel.

M105.name: Peat-cutting Expedition
M105.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M105.text: Immediately take as many moor tiles from your farmyard as you wish. For each moor tile that you remove, you receive 1 bonus point and 2 fuel. If you have at least 2 horses, you receive 1 additional fuel for each horse except the first one, up to a maximum of 4 additional fuel. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M106.name: Riverside Woods
M106.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M106.text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" action space, you receive 1 additional wood for each of your farmyard spaces with a forest tile, up to a maximum of 3 wood.

M107.name: Timber Mill
M107.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M107.text: Whenever you use the "Fell Trees" special action or take at least 4 wood from an action space, you receive 1 additional wood. If you pay 1 fuel, you receive 1 more additional wood.

M108.name: Oak Bark
M108.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M108.text: You immediately receive 2 wood. Whenever you convert cattle, wild boar, or horses to food, you receive 1 additional wood for each of these animals that you convert.

M109.name: Loam
M109.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M109.text: Whenever you have placed all except one of your people during the work phase and you use the "Hiring Fair" special action, you receive 1 additional clay.

M110.name: Alder Swamp
M110.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M110.text: Whenever you use a "Fell Trees" special action, you also receive your choice of 1 reed or 1 wood.

M111.name: Stone Wagon
M111.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M111.text: Whenever you use the "Day Labourer" action, you also receive 1 stone.

M112.name: Wheelbarrow
M112.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M112.text: Whenever you take at least 4 of the same building resources from an action space, you also receive 1 fuel. Whenever you "Cut Peat", you also receive 1 building resource of your choice.

M113.name: Peat Boat
M113.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M113.text: At any time, you can exchange fuel for goods as follows: 3 fuel → 2 wood or 2 clay; 4 fuel → 2 reed or 2 stone; 2 fuel → 1 grain; 3 fuel → 1 vegetable.

M114.name: Household Inventory
M114.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M114.text: If you have 5/6/7/8/9/10 empty farmyard spaces, you receive the first 1/2/3/4/5/6 goods from the following list (in order): 1 reed, 1 grain, 1 cattle, 1 stone, 1 vegetable, 1 horse. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M115.name: Village Shop
M115.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M115.text: Place 3 usage counters on this card. At any time, you can remove them to receive 1 other good or marker. The offer changes each round: 2: food, 3: grain, 4: fuel, 5: wood, 6: clay, 7: sheep, 8: reed, 9: wild boar, 10: stone, 11: cattle, 12: horse, 13: vegetable, 14: guest marker.

M116.name: Hearth Brush
M116.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M116.text: If the Stone Oven A6 has not yet been built, you can immediately place it underneath the Heating Stove M008 . From next round, whenever you use a person to take an action, you can build the Heating Stove immediately afterwards by paying the required resources. The Heating Stove is worth 1 additional bonus point for you.

M117.name: Pastoral Letter
M117.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M117.text: If the Well A10 has not yet been built, you can immediately place it under the Village Church M009 . From next round, whenever you use a person to take an action, you can build the Village Church immediately afterwards by paying the required resources. The Village Church and the Church M132 are each worth 1 additional bonus point for you.

M118.name: Co-operative Store
M118.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M118.text: Place 4 usage counters on this card. At any time, you can remove 1 of them to exchange any 1 building resource for any other building resource except stone.

M119.name: Out on the Wallaby
M119.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M119.text: You receive goods if you own craftman's buildings. Joinery A7: 3 wood; Pottery A8: 3 clay; Basketmaker's Workshop A9: 2 reeds. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

M120.name: Chamber of Commerce
M120.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M120.text: You immediately receive 1 wood and 1 reed. At the end of the game, each of these major improvements: Joinery A7, Pottery A8 and Basketmaker's Workshop A9 is worth 1 additional bonus point to you.

M121.name: Routine Work
M121.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M121.text: Whenever you choose not to use a craftman's building to convert building resources to food during the harvest, you receive your choice of 1 food or 1 fuel instead.

M122.name: Peat-Cutting Rights
M122.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M122.text: Add 4 and 7 to the current round. Place 1 moor tile on each of the corresponding rounds. These tiles show that you can use the special action "Cut Peat" at the start of the round.

M123.name: Agricultural Implement
M123.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M123.text: Whenever you use a person to take the "Plough fields" action, you can take a special action card immediately afterwards, as long as it has not already been used twice.

M124.name: Moor Enclosures
M124.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M124.text: Place 2 Moor tiles on this card. The card represents 2 additional, normal farmyard spaces. Place it beside your farmyard. Each of the new spaces must border an existing farmyard space. This expands your farm by 2 spaces.

M125.name: Bog Forest
M125.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M125.text: Immediately place 1 forest tile on as many of your own moor tiles as you wish. You can only use the covered moor tiles once you have removed the forest tiles with the "Fell Trees" special action. You cannot use "Slash and Burn" on forest tiles that are on top of moor tiles.

M126.name: Forest Swamp
M126.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M126.text: Whenever you use the "Cut Peat" special action, turn over the moor tile that you remove. Add 4 to the current round and place the tile on the corresponding round space. At the start of the round, you can place the forest tile on an empty space in your farmyard.

M127.name: Tree Nursery
M127.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M127.text: Place 1 forest tile on the spaces for rounds 12 and 13. At the start of these rounds, you can take the tile and place it on an empty space in your farmyard.

M128.name: Land Parcel
M128.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M128.text: When you play this card, you receive 1 forest tile that you must immediately place on an empty space in your farmyard. At the end of the game, you receive 2 bonus point if you have 1 unused farmyard space, you receive -1 bonus point if you have 2 unused spaces, and you receive -3 bonus point if you have 3 or more unused spaces.

M129.name: Stock-breeding Prize
M129.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M129.text: At the end of the game, you receive 2 bonus points if you have at least 1 animal of each of the 4 types.

M130.name: Family Burial Plot
M130.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M130.text: Immediately place the tombstone on an unused space in your farmyard. That space cannot be used to hold anything now, but is counted as used, and is worth 1 additional bonus point at the end of the game.

M131.name: Moor Archaeology
M131.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M131.text: Whenever you use the "Cut Peat" special action, you can place 1 fence from your supply on the emptied farmyard space. You cannot use this farmyard space to hold anything, but it is counted as used, and is worth 1 additional bonus point at the end of the game.

M132.name: Church
M132.reason: requires Farmers of the Moor systems (fuel/heating, horses, forest/moor tiles)
M132.text: For upgrading the Village Church M009 to the Church, you receive 2 food. At the end of each round, you can tithe up to 1 fuel to earn 1 bonus point.

NL001.name: Dutch Windmill
NL001.text: Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action in a round immediately following a Harvest, you receive 2 additional Food.

NL002.name: Parlor
NL002.text: Immediately change 1 room at no cost into a Stone room. (You will never need to renovate this room. Your home is still considered the material of your other rooms.)

NL003.name: Year of Disaster
NL003.text: Between the next Harvest and the Harvest after that, each other player must pay 1 Food to the general supply at the start of each Work phase. If they cannot pay 1 Food, they receive 1 negative point.

NL004.name: Cheese Market
NL004.text: At the end of each round that does not end with a Harvest, the player with the most dairy animals receives 1 Grain. (Dairy animals are Sheep and Cattle). Cattle count double in this calculation. If you tie for the most, only you receive the Grain. If two other players tie or no one has dairy animals, no one receives the Grain.

NL005.name: Studio
NL005.text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you can use the Studio to convert either 1 Wood, Clay, or Stone into Food. You receive 2 Food for Wood or Clay and 3 Food for Stone.

NL006.name: Backyard
NL006.text: Any 2x2 Unused farmyard space, where 1 of the spaces is orthogonally adjacent to your home, provides room for 1 person. (Once one of the spaces becomes used, move the Family member back to your supply or your home if you have room.)

NL007.name: Stage
NL007.text: Whenever you have a person on all 3 gameboards at the end of a Work phase, you receive 1 Food.

NL008.name: Water House
NL008.text: In the Replenish phase of each round (Phase 2), place 1 additional Food on the "Fishing" Action space. Whenever another player uses the "Fishing" Action space, they must pay you 2 Food from that Action space.

NL009.name: Junk Room
NL009.text: Whenever you play an Improvement (including this one), you receive 1 Food afterward.

NL010.name: Love Letter
NL010.text: If you use a "Family growth" action 1/2/3 rounds after playing this card, you also receive 2/3/5 Food. (Mark the 3rd round with an Arrow marker.)

NL011.name: Spittoon
NL011.text: Steal 2 Food markers from your left or right neighbor (your choice) and give them this card which they add to their hand. (*Rounds without Harvest are 1,2,3,5,6,8,10, and 12)

NL012.name: Brook
NL012.text: Whenever you use the "Take 3 Wood", "Take 1 Clay", or "Take 1 Reed" Action spaces located above the "Fishing" Action space, you receive 1 additional Food.

NL013.name: Grasshopper Bundles
NL013.text: In the Breeding phase of each Harvest, if you breed Sheep/ Wild boar/ Cattle and you have exactly 0-3/4-7/8+ planted Grain, you may receive 1 additional baby Sheep/ Wild Boar/ Cattle.

NL014.name: Ditch
NL014.text: When you Sow, you can plant Vegetables between orthogonally adjacent field tiles as though the gaps were fields. (These gaps do not count as fields when scoring.)

NL015.name: Row Boat
