occ_animal_tamer.text: Instead of just 1 animal total, you can keep 1 animal in each room of your house.

occ_baker.name: Baker
occ_baker.text: Each time you bake bread, you get 1 additional food per grain baked.

occ_braggart.name: Braggart
occ_braggart.text: During scoring, you get 2/3/4/5/7/9 bonus points for having at least 5/6/7/8/9/10 improvements in front of you.

occ_carpenter.name: Carpenter
occ_carpenter.text: Each new room costs you only 3 of the appropriate building resource (and 2 reed).

occ_cattle_feeder.name: Cattle Feeder
occ_cattle_feeder.text: Each time you take cattle from an action space, you get 1 additional cattle.

occ_clay_digger.name: Clay Digger
occ_clay_digger.text: Each time you take clay from an action space, you get 1 additional clay.

occ_conjurer.name: Conjurer
occ_conjurer.text: Each time you use the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you also get 1 wood and 1 grain.

occ_cook.name: Cook
occ_cook.text: At any time, you can convert grain to 2 food and vegetables to 3 food.

occ_deaconess.name: Deaconess
occ_deaconess.text: As long as you have exactly 2 family members, in the field phase of harvest 1/2/3/4/5/6, you get 1 wood and 1/2/3/4/5/6 food.

occ_estate_manager.name: Estate Manager
occ_estate_manager.text: At the end of the game, if no player has more animals of 1/2/3 types than you (and you have at least 1 of the type), you get 2/4/5 bonus points.

occ_firewood_collector.name: Firewood Collector
occ_firewood_collector.text: Each time you use the "Grain Seeds", "Farmland", "Grain Utilization", or "Cultivation" action space, you also get 1 wood.

occ_fisherman.name: Fisherman
occ_fisherman.text: Each time you use the "Fishing" action space, you get 2 additional food.

occ_forager.name: Forager
occ_forager.text: Each time you use the "Forest" accumulation space, you also get 1 food.

occ_grain_farmer.name: Grain Farmer
occ_grain_farmer.text: Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you get 1 additional grain.

occ_greengrocer.name: Greengrocer
occ_greengrocer.text: Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you also get 1 vegetable.

occ_hedge_keeper.name: Hedge Keeper
occ_hedge_keeper.text: Each time you build fences, up to 3 of them are free.

occ_house_steward.name: House Steward
occ_house_steward.text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 complete rounds left to play, you immediately get 1/2/3/4 wood. During scoring, each player with the most rooms gets 3 bonus points.

occ_market_crier.name: Market Crier
occ_market_crier.text: Each time you use the "Vegetable Seeds" action space, you get 1 additional vegetable.

occ_pig_breeder.name: Pig Breeder
occ_pig_breeder.text: Each time you take wild boar from an action space, you get 1 additional wild boar.

occ_quarryman.name: Quarryman
occ_quarryman.text: Each time you use the "Western Quarry" or "Eastern Quarry" accumulation space, you also get 1 food.

occ_reed_collector.name: Reed Collector
occ_reed_collector.text: Each time you take reed from an action space, you get 1 additional reed.

occ_roofer.name: Roofer
occ_roofer.text: When you renovate, you do not need to pay reed.

occ_seasonal_worker.name: Seasonal Worker
occ_seasonal_worker.text: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you also get 1 grain.

occ_shepherd.name: Shepherd
occ_shepherd.text: Each time you take sheep from an action space, you get 1 additional sheep.

occ_small_scale_farmer.name: Small-Scale Farmer
occ_small_scale_farmer.text: As long as you live in a house with exactly 2 rooms, you get 1 wood at the start of each round.

occ_stable_hand.name: Stable Hand
occ_stable_hand.text: Each time you build one or more stables, you also get 1 food per stable.

occ_stonecutter.name: Stonecutter
occ_stonecutter.text: Every room, renovation, and major improvement costs you 1 stone less.

occ_tutor.name: Tutor
occ_tutor.text: Each occupation you play after this one costs you 1 food less.

occ_water_carrier.name: Water Carrier
occ_water_carrier.text: At the start of each round, you get 1 food.

occ_woodcutter.name: Woodcutter
occ_woodcutter.text: Each time you take wood from an action space, you get 1 additional wood.

\u00d601.name: Big Backer
\u00d601.text: Whenever another player receives 2 or more animals of one type, you can buy one from him. A sheep costs 2 food, a wild boar 3 food, a cattle 4 food.

\u00d602.name: Action Artist
\u00d602.text: Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" action on an action space, you can return 1 animal of your choice to the general supply and receive 4 food in exchange.

\u00d603.name: Therapist
\u00d603.text: When you play this card, you can pay 2 food to immediately play 1 further occupation. Whenever another player plays an occupation, you can pay him 1 food. He must then, if possible, play a different occupation card instead.

\u00d604.name: Ski Instructor
\u00d604.text: Whenever another player has family growth, he must pay you 1 food (or beg the food, if necessary). You must immediately turn the Ski Instructor face-down when you grow your family. You no longer receive its benefits, but it still counts as a played occupation.

\u00d605.name: Lovable Vagabond
\u00d605.text: Whenever you have no more food during the feeding phase of a harvest and should take one or more begging cards, you receive one fewer begging card.

\u00d606.name: Opinion Leader
\u00d606.text: At the start of each round, you can mark one action space of your choice with an arrow (claim) marker. Each player can use this action space during this round. (This cannot be used on action spaces on which goods are placed). Remove the arrow marker at the end of each round.

\u00d607.name: Building Tycoon
\u00d607.text: Whenever another player builds at least one room, you can give him 1 food and immediately build exactly one room yourself.

\u00d608.name: Environmentalist
\u00d608.text: Whenever you play or buy an improvement, you can replace 1 building resource of your choice with 1 wood.

\u00d609.name: Family Singers
\u00d609.text: During the action phase, you can place any number of people on the family singers space instead of on an action space. For each person you place on the space, you receive 1 bonus point.

\u00d610.name: Waltzer
\u00d610.text: You can place 2 people one after another, as long as both action spaces are on the same board and adjoin one another (not diagonally).

\u00d611.name: Magnate
\u00d611.text: When you play this card, you immediately receive 2 stone. At the end of the game, the player(s) with the most building resources receive(s) 3 bonus points.

\u00d612.name: Child Prodigy
\u00d612.text: Whenever you have family growth, you can immediately pay 1 wood for 1 bonus point, or 2 wood for 2 bonus points.

\u00d613.name: Coffee House
\u00d613.text: The Coffee House is an action space for all players. Whenever a player uses the Coffee House, that player and the card's owner each receive 1 food from the general supply. In the next round, the person from this space can be placed before the starting player places his first person.

\u00d614.name: Spielefest
\u00d614.text: The Spielefest is an action space for all players. Whenever a player visits the Spielefest, he pays the owner of this card 1 food and receives 2 bonus points.

\u00d615.name: Farmers' Ball
\u00d615.text: When this card is played, each player who immediately pays 5 food into the general supply receives 3 bonus points.

\u00d616.name: Neutrality
\u00d616.text: In the next round, you can only place your people after the other players have placed all of theirs. For each person that you place late, you receive 1 bonus point.

\u00d617.name: Guest Worker
\u00d617.text: Take a Guest marker. From the next round, you can place this as you would a family member. The guest worker can use the most recent round card even if it has already been taken. He occupies a room in your home and must be fed with 2 food during the feeding phase of each harvest.

\u00d618.name: Schnitzel Mallet
\u00d618.text: For each cattle that you turn to food using a cooking improvement, you receive 1 additional food and 1 bonus point.

\u00d619.name: Compulsory Education
\u00d619.text: Whenever you have family growth, you can pay 1 food to immediately play an occupation.

\u00d620.name: Emergency Housing
\u00d620.text: Select 1 of your built stables. It is now a room for one of your family members. The stable loses its original function, but it is still worth 1 additional point if fenced.

\u00d621.name: Vineyard
\u00d621.text: Whenever you use the "Sow" action, you can plant up to 3 food on this card. Up to 3 stacks of food may be planted on it in total. These food are treated in the same way as sowed grain and are harvested during the harvest.

\u00d622.name: Unusual Architecture
\u00d622.text: Whenever you extend your home with 1 or more rooms, you receive 2 food for each new room.

\u00d623.name: Lederhosen
\u00d623.text: For each sheep that you convert to food, place 1 food from the general supply on this card. If, at the end of the game, you have at least as many food on the Lederhosen as you have family members, you receive 2 bonus points; if you have twice as many, you receive 3 bonus points.

\u00d624.name: Styrian Oak
\u00d624.text: Place 5 food from the general supply on the Styrian Oak. At the start of each round, you can return 1 food to the general supply. Once during the game, at any time, you can pay the number of food (0 to 5) remaining on the Styrian Oak to receive 6 wood from the supply.

\u010c01.name: Governor
\u010c01.text: Immediately take the top 4 occupations from the face-down deck, and choose 2 of them. You may play one of them immediately (taking into account the requirements on the card) without cost, and add the other one to your hand. If you don't want to play any, add both occupations to your hand.

\u010c02.name: Weekend Worker
\u010c02.text: Whenever you renovate your home, you may at the same time build 1 room. This costs you 1 reed, 1 of the old construction material of your home before renovating (wood or clay), and 2 of the new construction material (clay or stone).

\u010c03.name: Jack-of-all-trades
\u010c03.text: Immediately discard all the occupations that you have played. In every round, you can pay 1 food to use an occupation played by another player. You receive all benefits, except for those for playing the card and those at the end of the game. Discard the Jack-of-all-trades when you play another occupation.

\u010c04.name: Teacher of Nations
\u010c04.text: All players can pay 1 food less whenever they play an occupation. Whenever one of the other players uses this discount, you receive 1 food from the general supply.

\u010c05.name: Hero
\u010c05.text: You receive 3 food immediately. Once during the game, you can pay 1 wild boar to carry out a family growth action without placing one of your people. You must have room in your home. The newborn can start taking actions in the next round.

\u010c06.name: Fortune Teller
\u010c06.text: You immediately receive 1 stone from the general supply. Immediately look at the top 3 cards of the round card deck, and return them face down to the top of the deck, in any order you choose. You may look at the action card of the next round at any time in the game.

\u010c07.name: Bureaucrat
\u010c07.text: Place up to 3 food from your personal supply on this card. Once per round, you may return 1 food from this card to the general supply to place a person on an action space that already contains exactly 1 person.

\u010c08.name: Bagpiper
\u010c08.text: You receive 2 food immediately. The person who took the occupation action immediately returns home, as if he has not been used this round. The action space "Occupation" is now unoccupied, and can be used again.

\u010c09.name: Only Child
\u010c09.text: You receive wood immediately when you play this card: if played in round 9, 10, or 11, take 1 wood; if played in round 6, 7, or 8, take 2 wood; if played before round 6, take 3 wood. At the end of the game, you receive 3 bonus points if you have exactly 3 family members.

\u010c10.name: Informer
\u010c10.text: Immediately turn 1 of your occupations before you face down. You receive your choice of 5 food or 1 cattle from the general supply. You lose all effects of the face down occupation.

\u010c11.name: Robber
\u010c11.text: Once per round, you can skip placing a family member when it's your turn. Instead, you take your choice of 1 food or 1 wood from the general supply. You can only skip your turn if at least one other player still has at least one person left to place.

\u010c12.name: Nicholas
\u010c12.text: Immediately take 1 food from the general supply for each family member that has been born during the game so far, but no more than 6 food. All players with the least number of offspring (possibly including you) get 1 vegetable each.

\u010c13.name: Golden Hands
\u010c13.text: Whenever you play a minor or major improvement, you can replace exactly 1 building resource with another.

\u010c14.name: School
\u010c14.text: Whenever you can play 1 minor or major improvement, you can play 1 occupation instead. The price of the occupation is 1 food.

\u010c15.name: Beer Festival
\u010c15.text: You can bake bread immediately when you play this card. At the end of the game, you get 1 bonus point for every 3 food you have left, with a maximum of 3 points.

\u010c16.name: Wagenburg
\u010c16.text: You may place 1 fence on each of any number of your unused farmyard spaces. This costs 1 wood per fence. You may at any time return a fence from a farmyard space to your personal supply. Spaces with fences count as used at the end of the game.

\u010c17.name: Chapel
\u010c17.text: The Chapel is an additional action space. Whenever a player uses it, they can discard up to 2 begging cards. Every begging card is paid with 1 stone, which is placed on the Chapel. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each stone on this card.

\u010c18.name: Hop Field
\u010c18.text: You can immediately pay 1 to 4 wood to place that many fences on one of your empty fields. This field is no longer suitable for sowing, but will be scored as a field at the end of the game. For each fence on the field you receive 1 food immediately and 1 bonus point at the end of the game.

\u010c19.name: Wine Cellar
\u010c19.text: Immediately convert one of your unfenced stables to a wine cellar by turning it on its side. You can no longer keep animals in it. You receive 2 food from the general supply in the field phase of each harvest. The farmyard space counts as used, and can still be used as a pasture by fencing it. The wine cellar does not count as a stable.

\u010c20.name: Golem
\u010c20.text: Place the 2 clay that you paid on this card. At the start of each of the next 2 rounds, return 1 of the clay to the general supply. In these 2 rounds, you use the action space where you place your first person, twice in succession. For accumulating action spaces, this means that for the second action you receive the number of resources or food that is added at the start of each round.

\u010c21.name: Venus Figure
\u010c21.text: Whenever you use the action space "Family Growth and Minor Improvement", you may carry out the family growth action twice in the same turn, using only 1 person.

\u010c22.name: Bible
\u010c22.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, each family member that has used one of the following action spaces in the preceding round consumes 1 fewer food: "Plough 1 Field", "Plough Field and Sow", "Take 1 Grain", "Take 1 Vegetable", and "Day Labourer".

\u010c23.name: Hunger Wall
\u010c23.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, each player can pay 1 clay instead of 2 food to feed exactly 1 family member. Each paid clay is placed on this card. You receive 1 bonus point for every 2 clay on this card at the end of the game.

\u010c24.name: Five-year Plan
\u010c24.text: Place a claim marker on an empty field. You cannot sow again on this field. It no longer counts as a field, but only as a used farmyard space. You may immediately sow on all of your other empty fields. Put 2 extra grain or vegetables on each stack that you plant.
